<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Quelaunt](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3159)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

This three-armed, three-legged monster has no discernible eyes, nose, ears, or mouth, and no visible means of ingesting food. Its limbs are distributed so evenly across its body that it's all but impossible to tell which way the creature is oriented at any given time. Few who witness a quelaunt linger on its alien looks for long though, as the invasion of their minds becomes a more pressing concern, sowing doubt, sorrow, and rage. This monstrosity not only delights in the negative emotions of its prey, but feeds on them, gaining strength and sustenance from their dismay. For the quelaunt, no act is too terrible or cruel to inflict on its victims, since the more a creature suffers, the more the quelaunt feasts.

 Quelaunts are known to associate with other aberrant horrors, including [jah-tohls](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2930) and other creatures associated with the [Dominion of the Black](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=394). However, the more prevalent theory paints them as more alien—perhaps invasive beings from another dimension of pure thought and feeling—and claims that in their natural state they have no physical bodies at all. Few dare speculate further; the only known autopsy of a quelaunt resulted in the researcher's suicide just days afterward and all the notes were mysteriously destroyed. Whatever secrets there are to be unlocked in the anatomy of these bizarre monsters are apparently important enough to warrant great protection from quelaunts, even after death.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 34

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29
</column>

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 15">[Quelaunt](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3159)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=523" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 285

**Perception** +29; [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); (can't speak any language); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +27

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +5

**Con** +4

**Int** +5

**Wis** +6

**Cha** +8
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 36 

**Fort** +27 

**Ref** +26 

**Will** +31 (+33 vs. [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590))

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 305

</row>

**Resistances**
[mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 15

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet; [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 3d8+12 slashing

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 39
- **2nd**
[Laughing Fit](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583)
- **3rd**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) (at will)
- **7th**
[Wave of Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757) (×3)
- **Constant (4th)**
[Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534)

**Emotional Focus**   The quelaunt can cast the following cleric domain spells as 8th-rank occult innate spells at will without spending Focus Points: [_captivating adoration_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1829), [_delusional pride_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1787), and [_ignite ambition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1782).

**Emotional Frenzy** <actions string="Three Actions" />  The quelaunt casts up to three spells chosen from its at-will innate spells and its emotional focus spells.

**Feed on Emotion** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The quelaunt feeds on the emotional unrest of a single creature within 30 feet that's under a harmful [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) effect. The target must succeed at a DC 37 Will save or take 4d10 mental damage and be [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) for 1 round. If the target fails its saving throw, the quelaunt regains the same number of Hit Points and regains the action it spent to Feed on Emotion. It can't use the regained action to Feed on Emotion again.

**Rapid Strikes** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) The quelaunt makes three melee Strikes, each against a different target within reach. The multiple attack penalty applies to each attack but increases only after all the attacks have been made.

**Spiral of Despair** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** A creature fails a saving throw to resist one of the quelaunt's innate spells or emotional focus spells; **Effect** As the quelaunt invades the triggering creature's mind and plants the seeds of negative emotions, it also strips away the target's feelings of hope or positivity. The quelaunt can immediately end a single [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) effect from which the triggering creature is benefiting.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Other Quelaunts</title>

Perhaps the most frightening tales of quelaunts attribute them with abilities beyond those detailed here, suggesting that quelaunts might be an entire category of alien menace that has only recently turned its awful attention to humanity.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Raktavarna](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3160)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Raktavarnas are simple rakshasa incarnations of betrayal and assassination, often existing to test the mettle of those who were treacherous or poisonous in a previous life. They typically appear as a snake with blood-red eyes and oversized fangs, though they just as often appear in the guise of a sword or a piece of jewelry.

<actions string="Single Action" />

- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485)
- **1st**

- **4th**

**Betraying Bite**   A raktavarna gains a +2 bonus to Strikes against any creature that is holding it.

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The raktavarna takes on the appearance of a Tiny inanimate object. If, while transformed, the raktavarna takes any action other than the purely mental (such as [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367)), they immediately revert to their original form. Until then, they can use [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) the object.

**Designate Master**  ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=595)) The raktavarna spends 10 minutes on an invocation alongside another creature. That creature becomes the raktavarna's master until the raktavarna dies or Dismisses the effect. The master gains the Master's Eyes activity as long as the bond lasts.

**Master's Eyes** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689)) The master observes the world through the raktavarna's eyes instead of their own, using the raktavarna's Perception and [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59). This lasts until the end of the master's next turn, but the master can Sustain the activity. This ability functions at any range, even on different planes of existence. If the raktavarna dies while their master is using this ability, the master is [stunned 5](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93).

**Raktavarna Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 16 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d4 poison damage and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d4 poison damage and stupefied 2 (1 round).

</column>

The most iconic rakshasas, raja-krodhas are tiger-headed hunters of mortalkind. They are incarnations of all the malice people try to deny within themselves and instead wrongly ascribe to deadly predators of the wild. Their power and skill inspire fear, but also awe, and it is not unknown for some peoples to treat such a rakshasa as a guardian, if one to be treated with extreme caution. Despite their nature as brutal flesh-eaters, rajas are extremely eloquent and philosophical when they choose to be. This is simply another form of camouflage, one that allows them to blend into cities, much as their stripes allow them to fade into jungles, and it often lulls scholars and intellectuals into a false sense of security. While it is not in the nature of a raja-krodha to be a social schemer or a mastermind, it pleases them when others delude themselves into thinking they are. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Raja-Krodha](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3161) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 287 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +21, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +19, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +23 **Str** +6 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +5 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) taravari_ (functions as a [scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=393)) --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +20 **Will** +18 +2 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 180 **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10, Immunities [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) **Knowledge of Delusion** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A creature that fails a [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) check or a Perception check to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) on a rakshasa is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the end of its next turn.
**Reassert Fate** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet uses a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) or [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect; **Effect** The raja-krodha reasserts the ebb and flow of fate, instilling a deep dread in those who would attempt to cheat their written role. They disrupt the triggering effect, and the triggering creature becomes [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) and is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the raja-krodha until the end of its next turn. --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** taravari +23 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d6+12 slashing **Melee** fangs +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d6+12 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** claw +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d4+12 slashing **Divine Innate Spells** DC 29 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) - **3rd** [Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553) - **4th** [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466), [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732), [Vampiric Feast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1736) - **5th** [Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=1478), [Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=1551), [Invoke Spirits](/Spells.aspx?ID=1578) **Cleric Domain Spells (2 Focus Points),** DC 29 - **5th** [Ignite Ambition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1782), [Savor the Sting](/Spells.aspx?ID=1826) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The raja-krodha takes on the appearance of any Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628). This doesn't change the raja-krodha's Speed or their attack and damage modifiers with their Strikes but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning). They lose their fangs Strike unless the humanoid form has fangs or a similar unarmed attack. **Cruel Majesty** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Requirements** The rakshasa is not in its true form; **Effect** The rakshasa Changes Shape into its true form in a display that is equal parts terrifying and majestic. Creatures within 30 feet of the rakshasa must succeed at a DC 29 Will save or be [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the rakshasa until the beginning of the rakshasa's next turn as they are awestruck. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The raja-krodha deals 2d6 extra precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) Medium, 2d12+6 bludgeoning, Rupture 15

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Giant Rat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3162)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Giant rats are enormous versions of the common vermin. They are typically found in abundant numbers, but since they cannot fit in the nooks where mundane rats typically hide, they are much easier to locate and exterminate. They mostly live in sewers where they can scavenge from the streets above, but some families of giant rats live in more remote locations, such as dank caves, forests or hills. Rats are incredibly adept survivors and can be found nearly anywhere in the world, though they tend to favor temperate or warm climates as opposed to cold regions.

 Although its bite alone is not lethal except to the very young or very old, the giant rat carries the putrid plague common to rodents around the world—a pestilence more than capable of ravaging rural communities.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Rat Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3163)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A swarm of rats can cause total chaos within a household or business. Contracting putrid plague is all the easier when dozens of these agitated or hungry vermin gather to bite victims en masse, making rat-hunting a viable career in many parts of the world as desperate townspeople seek relief from the disease's spread.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Rat Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3163)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Swarm" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=707" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 288

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Rats and Disease</title>

Rats have a reputation of being vicious, aggressive animals that attack food stores in great numbers and spread disease. While rats are immune to the most severe effects of their own putrid plague, the disease makes them unpredictable and aggressive. Some populations of rats carry even more deadly diseases, such as [bubonic plague](/Diseases.aspx?ID=6).
</aside>

markdown:
Redcaps are sadistic and capricious fey who thrill in bloodletting and murder. While they are most famous for appearing as wizened, bearded men, redcaps of other genders are no less fearsome or cruel. However, redcaps are ultimately craven bullies, cowed by anything more powerful than themselves, a trait that leads them to fear and despise the symbols of deities. Many fairy tales explain how the redcaps draw power from dipping the hats for which they are named in fresh blood. Just as iconic to these cruel little fey are their iron-clad boots, and the clanging sound of their metal soles clanking on stone floors is both discordant and disconcerting—especially to those who recognize the sound for what it is. Redcaps typically stand only 3 feet tall, with hunched frames, pointed ears, crooked teeth, and long, white, tangled hair. Though unlikely to share true affection, redcaps find camaraderie in murder. Small troupes of redcaps travel together, sharing bloody kills and reminiscing on their brutal exploits. They loathe the company of most other creatures, with the notable exception of the feline fey called [elananxes](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2972). **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Redcap](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3165) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 290 **Perception** +12; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +13, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 **Red Cap** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) A redcap's woolen hat is dyed with the blood of their victims. If the redcap loses their cap, they no longer benefit from [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) and take a –4 status penalty to their damage rolls. They can create a new cap in 10 minutes, but that cap doesn't grant them powers until the redcap has turned it red with Blood Soak. A cap has no benefit for creatures other than the redcap who made it. **Items** [Halberd](/Weapons.aspx?ID=26), iron boots, red cap --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +15 **Will** +10 **HP** 60 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 10) **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5, divine revulsion **Divine Revulsion** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) If a redcap sees a creature brandish a religious symbol of a deity (which requires an Interact action by that creature) or cast a divine spell while wearing a religious symbol, the redcap must attempt a DC 19 Will save. They then become temporarily immune to all brandished religious symbols for 10 minutes.
**Critical Success** The redcap is unaffected. **Success** The redcap is [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76). **Failure** The redcap gains the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) condition for 1 round and is frightened 4. --- **Speed** 50 feet **Melee** halberd +15 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d10+10 slashing **Melee** iron boot +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [versatile B](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6+8 piercing **Blood Soak** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The redcap dips their cap in the blood of a slain foe. The foe must have died in the last minute, and the redcap must have helped kill it. The redcap gains a +4 status bonus to damage rolls for 1 minute. **Deadly Cleave** **Trigger** The redcap reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points with a halberd Strike; **Effect** The redcap makes another halberd Strike against a different creature, using the same multiple attack penalty as the halberd Strike that triggered this reaction. This counts toward their multiple attack penalty as normal. **Stomp** The redcap Strides up to half their Speed and makes a boot Strike at any point during that movement. If the boot Strike hits a [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) creature, it deals an extra 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
Stomping Ground Unlike some fey, redcaps don't embody a particular natural feature or environment, so they can be found almost anywhere. They tend to prefer areas that allow them to hide or at least get behind cover with their quick movement, such as forests, mountains, and underground tunnels and caverns. Sometimes they take up residence in abandoned buildings, especially barns and sheds.
name: Redcap

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Reefclaw](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3166)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Reefclaws are aquatic monsters that resemble huge shrimp or lobsters. As one might expect from its name, a reefclaw's oversized claws are powerful weapons with a viselike grip and the ability to inject potent venom into unfortunate prey.

 Though reefclaws can't speak, they are intelligent enough to understand the local language of humanoids near their hunting grounds. The creatures sometimes listen to conversations, either to gain intelligence on the best place to ambush aquatic or land-dwelling prey, or merely for entertainment value—reefclaws are particularly fond of listening to people with high-pitched voices. Reefclaws are usually solitary hunters, but small swarms of female reefclaws have been known to gather around a single male for mating purposes or together for the communal raising of their broods. In the latter case, the females will perform widespread hunts in order to bring back a large enough bounty to feed their young. Such hunting parties are extremely dangerous—they've been known to tip over small fishing boats and attack those who fall overboard.

 Once a reefclaw has decided upon a course of action, it follows through even if doing so spells its own end. More than one reefclaw survivor has said that the creature released its bone-crushing grasp only after the brains were leaking from its broken skull, and even then, the reefclaw was able to perform a terrible last slash as part of its dying breath. During mating season, female reefclaws are often a little more pragmatic and release their prey before endangering themselves and their offspring.

 Despite their intelligence and the accompanying moral quandaries, reefclaws frequently find their way onto the dinner plates of land-dwelling hunters such as humans and hobgoblins. According to those who have a taste for reefclaw flesh, the meat is either delectably sweet (for reefclaws raised in colder waters) or slightly tangy (in the case of warm-water reefclaws). Most people who know of reefclaws' intellect find the act of eating them distasteful, but this does not dissuade unscrupulous nobles in coastal regions, for whom reefclaw meat is a delicacy well worth the expense. Likewise, fishers whose focus is on the bottom line of their ledgers are more than willing to hunt the dangerous creatures—or, even better, hire out the task to naive adventurers.

</row>

**Reefclaw Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 17 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 4 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 round), **Stage 2** 1d6 poison damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Eating Reefclaws</title>

Reefclaws amass no treasure, but their meat—if kept fresh—can be sold in the right markets. However, in an increasing number of places, the practice of eating reefclaws has fallen out of favor—which means, of course, that the reefclaw market has simply shifted to black-market butchers, where all manner of meat gathered from dubious sources is available for purchase, and the value has increased accordingly.
</aside>
name: Reefclaw

markdown:
Revenants are obsessed, undead stalkers who arise from their own murders and are driven by only one thing: revenge against their killers. The common wisdom is that revenants arise only from individuals who have been utterly betrayed or abandoned to die a grueling death, but even then, such victims might not rise from their graves. In other cases, revenants might even rise from what might legitimately be considered an accident if the revenant doesn't understand the full circumstances of their demise. In such cases, it doesn't matter that the “murderer” may not have intended to kill, for revenants understands no pity and can never forgive. Revenants have little memory of their lives other than anything they might need to recall in order to achieve their goal of vengeance. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Revenant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3167) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 292 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), sense murderer **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); any one spoken in life by their murderer (typically [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155)) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +14 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Sense Murderer** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689)) A revenant knows the direction of their murderer (as long as both are on the same plane), but not the distance. --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +13 **Will** +17 **HP** 115 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** physical 5 (except slashing) **Self-Loathing** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) If a revenant sees their own reflection or any object that was important to them in life, they must attempt a DC 25 Will save.
**Critical Success** The revenant is unaffected and can no longer be affected by that reflection or object in this way.
**Success** The revenant is distracted by self-loathing and becomes [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) for 1 round.
**Failure** The revenant becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) by the source that triggered their self-loathing and does everything they can to destroy it until the end of the revenant's next turn.
**Critical Failure** The revenant becomes [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) as long as the source of their self-loathing is apparent, until they're attacked, or until they see their murderer.
**Undying Vendetta** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) If the revenant's murderer dies, the revenant is immediately destroyed. A revenant that can't sense their murderer must attempt a DC 11 flat check once every 24 hours to avoid becoming [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) and [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88); they immediately rise again once they can sense their murderer. A murderer who becomes [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) does not trigger the revenant's destruction until the murderer is finally destroyed. The revenant gains a +2 status bonus to checks and DCs against their murderer. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** claw +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d8+5 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Baleful Shriek** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The revenant wails horribly. Each creature within a 60-foot burst must attempt a DC 23 Will save. Regardless of the outcome of their saving throw affected creatures are then immune to Baleful Shriek for 1 hour. The revenant's murderer never improves their degree of success due to this ability's incapacitation trait. The revenant can't use Baleful Shriek again for 1d4 rounds. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76).
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2 and [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 3 and paralyzed for 1d4 rounds. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 2d6+5 bludgeoning, DC 24
Exceptions To Evil While most undead are indiscriminately malevolent, revenants are not—these unusual stalkers rise not out of a sense of cruelty or hatred of the living, but spontaneously from the need for vengeance following a deep betrayal. One can avoid a revenant's wrath by simply getting out of its way—unless you happen to be its reason for unlife!
name: Revenant

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Rhinoceros](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3168)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Rhinoceroses are short-tempered, territorial, and easily startled, and these traits combined with their innate ferocity means their natural instinct when disturbed is to attack. When intruders disturb or surprise rhinoceroses, they respond by charging directly at the interlopers and then lashing out with their mighty horns.

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 293

**Perception** +9; [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet

**Languages**

**Skills**

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Roc](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3170)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These legendary, massive raptors, capable of carrying off elephants as prey, are typically about 30 feet long from beak to tail and have a wingspan of 80 feet or more. While their beaks are hooked to rip flesh from bone, their hunting strategy involves grabbing their prey in their powerful talons and then dropping it from great heights before feeding. This method creates a massive amount of carrion, which guarantees that rocs are followed by flocks of opportunistic scavengers, such as ravens and buzzards, who find it easy to steal bits of the larger birds' meals. Rocs, for the most part, don't mind these creatures, which sometimes get gobbled up along with the rest of the roc's food.

 Rocs usually nest among mountaintops and cliffs inaccessible to all but the bravest of terrestrial dwellers. They are long-range predators that hunt both land and sea in search for massive prey to sustain them and their young. Rocs are antisocial and lone hunters who compete with each other in fierce aerial battles to protect territory. But about once a decade, a mating couple pairs up to raise their chicks. Once the chicks are old enough to hunt on their own, the parents separate to once again engage in lone hunting. Particularly skilled druids or rangers might capture and train a roc to serve as a flying mount or hunting companion, though examples of such an incredible feat of domestication are few and far between. The easiest way to rear a roc is to do so from the moment it hatches, since the chick imprints on the first creature it sees. Acquiring a roc egg is by no means an easy feat, though, and is often a death sentence for the would-be egg-snatcher.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

**Wing Rebuff** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature moves from beyond the reach of the roc's wing to within the reach of the roc's wing; **Effect** The roc makes a wing Strike against the triggering creature. If the roc [Pushes](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) the creature, it disrupts the triggering [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, fly 60 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
beak +21 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),

**Damage** 2d6+10 bludgeoning plus [Improved Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) 10 feet

**Carry**   A roc can [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) at half Speed while it has a creature grabbed or restrained in either or both of its talons, carrying that creature along with it.

**Flying Strafe** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The roc [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) up to its Speed and makes two talon Strikes at any point during that movement. Each Strike must target a different creature. Each attack takes the normal multiple attack penalty.

**Snack**   A roc gains a +2 circumstance bonus to hit with its beak Strike if the target is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) in its talon.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Roc's Riches</title>

While most treasure a roc may possess is incidental—the remains of prey haphazardly scattered in or around the nest—roc feathers, especially white or gold ones, are highly prized in certain markets. Even more valuable are roc eggs, especially to some [giants](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=420) who enjoy their unique flavor.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Sargassum Heap](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3171)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A sargassum heap is a mass of semi-intelligent seaweed that floats through the ocean, luring in its victims with hallucinogenic spores. Those affected by the spores are drawn towards the heap, envisioning their heart's desire. This might be a lost loved one, a child in need of help, an enchanting mermaid, the promise of dry land, and so on. Once their prey is close enough, the sargassum heap lashes out with its seaweed tendrils and crushes it to death.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Satyr](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3173)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

To a satyr, life is a party and everyone is invited. Notorious for their hedonism, these fey believe there's no greater beauty than can be found in song, drink, indulgent meals, and carnal pleasures. Satyrs use their enchanting songs and natural charm to encourage all manner of people to follow their true desires and free themselves from society's rules. This usually involves enticing mortals to join raucous parties or engage in trysts in moonlit glades. If a potential companion rejects a satyr's advances, however, the satyr has little interest in continuing a conversation and goes off to find more amenable revelers.

 The lifestyle of a satyr leaves no room for ongoing affairs or long-term friends. Once his party is over or his lust is satiated, the satyr disappears back into the forest. The offspring satyrs leave behind are satyrs themselves, and they usually end up being taken from their cradles by other fey rather than left in mortals' care. Satyrs are always male.

 The untouched beauty of the forest is sacred and precious to a satyr. Brutish intruders who clear-cut trees or massacre animals without eating them risk drawing a satyr's ire. A satyr so provoked uses his spells to undermine foes and attempts to dispatch them either with brutal ambushes or by leading a rush of forest animals to attack.

 Other fey, particularly more benevolent fey, look upon satyrs as loutish, embarrassing cousins. They're rarely hostile toward satyrs, but most find them insufferable and advise any mortals they like to steer clear of satyrs' glades.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

</column>

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +13, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +11, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +9, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +8

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +4

**Con** +1

**Int** +1

</row>

**Sylvan Wine** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) A satyr's wineskin magically enchants any alcohol inside. With an Interact action, a living creature can imbibe the alcohol and gain a +1 item bonus to Will saves and a +3 item bonus to Will saves against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effects for the following hour. When the wineskin is removed from a satyr's person, the magic remains only until the wine spoils. The wineskin holds up to eight drafts of wine.

**Items**
[Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), panpipes, [Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=77) (20 arrows), wineskin

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 19 

**Fort** +9 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +12 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 80

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortbow +14 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d6 +3 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 piercing

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 21
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Courageous Anthem](/Spells.aspx?ID=1763), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Triple Time](/Spells.aspx?ID=1778), [Uplifting Overture](/Spells.aspx?ID=1779)
- **4th**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693)

**Fleet Performer**   When the satyr Plays the Pipes to cast a spell, he can Step or Stride as part of the activity.

**Play the Pipes** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Requirements** The satyr is holding a [musical instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2737); **Effect** The satyr plays a melody on his instrument to cast charm, [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675), or [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) without expending the spell slot. The spell gains the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait and targets all creatures in a 60-foot emanation instead of its usual targets. A creature that succeeds at its Will save against any spell is then temporarily immune from spells played from that satyr's pipes for 1 minute. Satyrs are immune to this music.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Satyr Pipes</title>

A satyr's gear is valuable, particularly his fine panpipes. Depending on the type of music they prefer to play, satyrs may be able to cast different 4th-rank spells, such as [_laughing fit_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583) or [_paranoia_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1623). Satyrs also stow caches of alcohol, rich food, and pieces of fine art (especially erotic art) in hollows and glades they frequent.
</aside>
name: Satyr

A ramshackle collection of materials in a human shape, the scarecrow construct is indistinguishable from a normal scarecrow until it slowly creaks to life. As it animates, its carved pumpkin or sackcloth face bursts into eldritch flame, sending fear creeping into the air around it. Each scarecrow is handcrafted and unique in its appearance, though most are 5 to 6 feet tall and constructed of a combination of wood, cloth, rope, straw, sawdust, discarded husks and cobs, and similar materials, all dressed in ragged pastoral garments. This rudimentary construction makes a scarecrow somewhat fragile, prone to snapping limbs in the crush of battle. Yet its structure is adaptable, allowing it to reshape another piece of itself into a clawed limb or grip a severed portion of itself to swat at its foes. When a scarecrow is created, it must be anointed with a drop of its creator's blood into each of its eyes. This blood soaks into the material and siphons a tiny sliver of the creator's soul away—not enough to harm the creator, but more than enough to imbue the scarecrow with an instinctive intellect that allows it to follow commands as eagerly as a well-trained (if ill-tempered) guard dog. When a scarecrow is destroyed, the blood leaks back out from its eyes, but the portion of the creator's soul never returns. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Scarecrow](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3174) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 297 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** -4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +8 **Will** +11 **HP** 60 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), poison, [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Resistances** physical 5 (except slashing) **Weaknesses** fire 5 **Scarecrow's Leer** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 40 feet. The scarecrow's eyes flicker with an unnerving glow. A creature can't reduce its [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition below 1 as long as it's in the aura. When a creature enters or starts its turn in the aura, it must attempt a DC 18 Will save. Birds and other avian creatures take a –2 circumstance penalty to this save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is then temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is frightened 1.
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2 and is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) by the scarecrow until the end of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but frightened 3. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** claw +13 ([versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d6+7 bludgeoning plus clawing fear **Baleful Glow** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The scarecrow's head bursts into ghostly, heatless flame that sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot emanation (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) to the next 20 feet). If the scarecrow uses this ability on the first round of combat, any creature that has not acted yet is startled, becoming [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against the scarecrow for 1 round. The scarecrow can suppress the light by using this action again. **Clawing Fear** The scarecrow's strikes deal an additional 1d6 mental damage to [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) creatures. **Mundane Appearance** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) Until it acts, the scarecrow resembles an ordinary scarecrow. It has an automatic result of 32 on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks and DCs to pass as an ordinary scarecrow.
Odds and Ends Scarecrows hold a certain liminal space that is inhabitable by spirit creatures. [Ghosts](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=418) in particular can use their malevolent possession on a scarecrow as if it were a living creature, using them as vehicles to escape their site-bound nature and so further spread their murder and mayhem.
name: Scarecrow

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Giant Scorpion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3175)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These massive, terrifying arachnids are typically 8 feet long from head to the base of the tail. Giant scorpions are the favored pack animals and war beasts of various desert-dwelling creatures, particularly [kholos](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=434). They are most commonly encountered in the wild, however. There they lair in mountainside caves or burrow beneath shallow layers of sand where they lie in wait for prey to wander near.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Sea Serpent](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3177)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These fabled beasts resemble massive snakes with long rows of finned spines down their back. Temperamental and territorial, sea serpents can capsize a boat with ease, and most won't hesitate to do so when hungry or threatened. Stories abound of aggrieved captains who spend their entire lives hunting down the elusive monster that sunk their ships and took their livelihoods. These hunts rely on rumors and glimpses of the beasts, as few survive the catastrophes wrought by sea serpents.

 While many fishermen's tales paint sea serpents as divinely appointed guardians of the ocean or as evil and demonic agents, the truth is that most sea serpents are simply very large beasts with a knack for avoiding magical detection.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 30

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Sea_Serpent.webp" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Dex** +4

**Con** +6

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Undetectable** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) A sea serpent automatically tries to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574), [revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=686), or [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689) effect attempted against it, using its [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) modifier for its counteract modifier.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 35 

**Fort** +25 

**Ref** +21 

**Will** +21 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 210

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 60 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +27 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 3d10+14 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d10+14 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
water spout +25 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)),
**Damage** 2d6+12 bludgeoning plus sea serpent algae

**Capsize** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) The sea serpent attempts to capsize an aquatic vessel of its size or smaller that it's adjacent to. It must succeed at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check with a DC of 35 (reduced by 5 for each size smaller than the sea serpent) or the pilot's [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41)DC, whichever is higher.

**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** <actions string="Single Action" />  1d10+14 bludgeoning, DC 32

**Sea Serpent Algae**  ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) The water in the ballast organs around the sea serpent's neck is full of psychotropic algae. **Saving Throw** DC 34 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) and, if flying, spends its first action each turn to descend 20 feet (1 round); **Stage 2** confused and, if flying, descends until reaching the ground or water below (1 round)

**Spine Rake** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) The sea serpent extends the spines along its back and [Swims](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) or Strides. Each creature the serpent is adjacent to at any point during its movement takes 4d6+8 slashing damage with a DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

**[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) Huge, 2d10+6 bludgeoning, Rupture 20

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Shipwreck Lairs</title>

While an underwater cave will do, sea serpents prefer to “build” lairs by sinking ships. A sea serpent might even create a massive underwater graveyard by crashing several ships in the same location and letting the debris stack up on the ocean floor.
</aside>
name: Sea Serpent

<row gap="medium">

Scouts, usually young sedacthies, ply the ocean in search of animal servants or tread ashore to hunt. Scouts hunting on the surface press [crocodiles](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2887) and [snakes](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=473) into service, while those underwater prefer [electric eels](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2970) and [hippocampi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3050).

By spending a week regularly interacting with such an animal, the sedacthy can make it permanently [helpful](/Conditions.aspx?ID=78). **Items** [Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10), [Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10) (2) --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +9 **Will** +9 **HP** 75 **Vengeful Throw** **Trigger** The marauder takes damage from a creature 20 feet or further away; **Effect** The marauder makes a ranged spear Strike against the triggering creature. This attack doesn't take a range increment penalty if the target is within the second range increment. --- **Speed** 20 feet, swim 40 feet **Melee** spear +14, **Damage** 1d6+10 piercing **Melee** jaws +14, **Damage** 1d4+8 piercing plus 1d4 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Melee** claw +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d4+8 slashing **Ranged** spear +11 ([thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d6+10 piercing **Challenging Shriek** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The marauder unleashes a terrifying battle cry. Each enemy in a 30-foot emanation must attempt a DC 21 Will save. Regardless of the results, creatures are temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76).
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) for 1 round and frightened 3. **Shared Feast** The sedacthy makes a jaws Strike. If it hits, an ally of their choice can spend a reaction to make a jaws Strike against the same target. Allies with beaks or similar attacks can use those instead of jaws.
name: Sedacthy Marauder

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Sedacthy Speaker](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3180)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

High-ranking sedacthies are expected to both plan campaigns and wade into battle. These speakers achieve their rank by accumulating several large servants or a single massive creature like a [megalodon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=366).

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Sedacthy Speaker](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3180)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Amphibious" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=529" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
<trait label="Sedacthy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=770" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 301

**Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [wavesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=84) 30 feet

**Languages**
[Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113); sea speech

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +16, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +14, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +15, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +15

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +3

**Con** +4

**Int** +2

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +5
</row>

**Sea Speech** A sedacthy speaking [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) can be understood by any [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) that has a swim Speed or the [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=529) or [aquatic](/Traits.aspx?ID=533) trait. By spending a week regularly interacting with such an animal, the sedacthy can make it permanently [helpful](/Conditions.aspx?ID=78).

**Items**
_+1 [trident](/Weapons.aspx?ID=401)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 23 

**Fort** +14 

**Ref** +13 

**Will** +15 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 95

</row>

**Speaker's Privilege** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The sedacthy speaker takes damage; **Requirements** The sedacthy speaker has [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) from an [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) ally; **Effect** The animal takes the damage instead.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_trident_ +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 1d8+10 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +16,
**Damage** 1d6+8 piercing plus 1d4 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 1d6+8 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_trident_ +14 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d8+8 piercing

**Animal Shield** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The sedacthy speaker is adjacent to a Large or larger [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) ally; **Effect** The speaker gains [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) until the start of their next turn or when they're no longer adjacent to the animal, whichever comes first.

**Exploit Weakness**   The speaker's Strikes deal 1d6 additional damage to creatures that are [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) or [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91).

**Painful Cry** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) The sedacthy shrieks across a range of painfully high tones, dealing 3d6 sonic damage and 1d6 mental damage to all creatures in a 30-foot cone, with a DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. A creature that fails its save is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91).

**Shared Feast** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The sedacthy makes a jaws Strike. If it hits, an ally of their choice can spend a reaction to make a jaws Strike against the same target. Allies with beaks or similar attacks can use those instead of jaws.

**Swim Together** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The speaker is adjacent to an [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) ally; **Effect** The speaker and the animal both [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381), ending their movement adjacent to one another.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Zyss Serpentfolk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3181)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Even the least among zyss serpentfolk consider themselves greater than any mammal. Their magical abilities, most notably their telepathy, are all the reason they need to hold this view. And it's true enough that the instinctual skill and magic of any zyss is enough to best the average human.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Zyss_Serpentfolk.webp" />
</column>

</row>

markdown:
Aapophs possess greater strength and stronger venom than their zyss kin, but they lack zyss's innate magic. Unlike their selfish superiors, aapophs are communal and group together to hunt, wrestle, and sleep curled together in pits. Aapophs often have physical mutations—horns, vestigial tails, or spines protruding from their scales—yet these have little impact on their overall combat prowess—and combat prowess is the measure by which zyss judge them. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Aapoph Serpentfolk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3182) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 303 **Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +6 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** -1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** -1 **Items** [Scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=38) --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +7 **Will** +6 (+2 status vs. [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **HP** 60 **Resistances** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 5 **[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** scimitar +11 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 1d6+6 slashing **Melee** fangs +11, **Damage** 1d8+6 piercing plus serpentfolk venom **Melee** tail +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 1d6+6 bludgeoning plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46) **Serpentfolk Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 20 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d4 poison damage and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d4 poison damage and enfeebled 1 (1 round) **Slithering Attack** The aapoph serpentfolk makes one scimitar or fangs Strike and one tail Strike, each targeting a different creature. These attacks both count toward the aapoph's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after the aapoph makes both attacks.
Aapoph Mutations Aapophs are prone to mutations, which you can choose or roll using a d%.
1–45No mutation
46–56 Dual tail
57–66 Additional fangs
67–84 Hooded neck
85–91 Horns
92–96 Additional, vestigial head
97–100 Spiny scales

markdown:
Some serpentfolk undergo intense ritual training and practice to improve their innate ability to disguise themselves. These serpentfolk often identify as members of a sinister society known as the Coils of Ydersius, and the most devoted of their number seek out methods of reincarnating into new forms to infiltrate enemy societies even more efficiently. Coil spies train in methods of infiltrating other societies to such an extent that they might be capable of infiltrating a mammalian civilization for years. Though they're expected to work entirely toward the eventual triumph of their people, most Coil spies also find personal pursuits. When Coil spies get caught, it's rarely due to a lack of skill, but rather to their arrogance or recklessness as they pursue their hedonistic desires. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Coil Spy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3183) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 304 **Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Gnomish](/Languages.aspx?ID=5), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +10, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +13, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +11, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +10, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +12 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +5 **Items** [Hand Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=69) (20 bolts), [Spider Venom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2906) (2), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43), [Thieves' Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758) --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +12 **Will** +10 (+4 status vs. [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 48 **Resistances** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 5 **Thin of Blood** Zyss serpentfolk recover slowly from injuries. When they take physical damage from a critical hit, they gain 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). They take a –2 circumstance penalty to flat checks to recover from persistent damage and saving throws against afflictions.
 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** shortsword +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6+5 piercing plus serpentfolk venom **Melee** fangs +14 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 1d6+5 piercing plus serpentfolk venom **Ranged** hand crossbow +10 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+3 piercing plus serpentfolk venom or spider venom **Occult Innate Spells** DC 21 - **1st** [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740) (at will) - **2nd** [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455) (self only; at will) - **3rd** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) (at will) - **4th** [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) **Deceptive Reposition** The Coil spy Strides up to half their Speed and attempts a [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390), in either order. **Maintain Disguise** A Coil spy can maintain an ongoing [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) as long as they are conscious without having to re-cast the spell; they need only Cast the Spell again to reassume their _illusory disguise_ if they wish to change their appearance or if the active spell is dispelled. Coil spies typically seek privacy when they need to sleep, as an ongoing _illusory disguise_ ends an hour after they fall unconscious. **Serpentfolk Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 19 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d4 poison damage and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d4 poison damage and enfeebled 1 (1 round) **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The Coil spy's Strikes deal an extra 2d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.

markdown:
The mutated aapophs dubbed granitescales have bulky frames covered in hard gray plates. These scales offer protection but shed when struck with too much force. Granitescales like to carve their shed scales into small chips and attach them as rattles to their spears. Many an unsuspecting victim has heard the hiss of a granitescale's rattle too late. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Aapoph Granitescale](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3184) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 304 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=371)) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +15 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** -1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +1 **Items** [Javelin](/Weapons.aspx?ID=71) (5), [Longspear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=6) --- **AC** 24 (22 with shed scales) **Fort** +16 **Ref** +14 **Will** +11 (+2 status vs. [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **HP** 120 **Resistances** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 5 **Chipping Scales** **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** The granitescale is about to take piercing or slashing damage; **Effect** The granitescale twists to take the blow on their hardest scales, which they shed to reduce the incoming force. The granitescale gains resistance 15 to the damage, but their AC is reduced by 2 for 1 day, when the shed scales regrow.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** longspear +17 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d8+11 piercing **Melee** fangs +17, **Damage** 1d8+11 piercing plus serpentfolk venom **Ranged** javelin +16 ([range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6+11 piercing **Rattling Spear** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Requirements** The granitescale's last action was a successful longspear Strike; **Effect** The granitescale rattles the base of their spear, attempting an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) all enemies within 30 feet (compare the check result to the targets' Will DCs individually). **Serpentfolk Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 22 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d4 poison damage and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d4 poison damage and enfeebled 1 (1 round)
name: Aapoph Granitescale

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Bone Prophet](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3185)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The speakers for the dead known as bone prophets hold an esteemed place as voices for their decapitated god. Burial rites, necromantic rituals, and the delivery of cryptic utterances supposedly whispered to them by [Ydersius](/Deities.aspx?ID=211) all fall under the dominion of these priests.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Bone_Prophet.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 8">[Bone Prophet](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3185)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
<trait label="Serpentfolk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=771" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 305

**Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +15, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +17, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +19, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** +5

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +6
</row>

**Items**
[invisibility potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2944), [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2745) of [Ydersius](/Deities.aspx?ID=211), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 27 

**Fort** +14 

**Ref** +15 

**Will** +19 (+4 status vs. [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647))

+1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 115

</row>

**Resistances**
[poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 10

**Thin of Blood** Zyss serpentfolk recover slowly from injuries. When they take physical damage from a critical hit, they gain 2d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). They take a –2 circumstance penalty to flat checks to recover from persistent damage and saving throws against afflictions.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_staff_ +18 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)),
**Damage** 2d4+9 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fangs +17 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 2d6+9 piercing plus serpentfolk venom

**Divine Spontaneous Spells** DC 28, attack +20
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745)
- **1st**
[Bane](/Spells.aspx?ID=1447), [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740) (4 slots)
- **2nd**
[Blood Vendetta](/Spells.aspx?ID=1454), [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) (4 slots)
- **3rd**
[Bind Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=1449), [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1453), [Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1464), [Vampiric Feast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1736) (4 slots)
- **4th**
[Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552), [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647), [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712) (3 slots)

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 28
- **1st**
[Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740) (at will)
- **2nd**
[Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455) (self only; at will)
- **3rd**
[Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) (at will)
- **5th**
[Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693)
- **6th**
[Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501)

**Rituals** DC 28

- **2nd**
[Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=10)

**Raise Serpent** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The bone prophet animates corpses of snakes, serpentfolk, or similar serpentine creatures within a 30-foot emanation. Any flesh on the bodies sloughs off, and they rise as [skeletons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=472). The bone prophet can raise one Large creature as a [skeletal giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=375) or up to three Medium creatures as [skeletal champion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=373); the equipment and attacks might be different depending on the corpses' possessions. These skeletons have the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=653) trait and are under the bone prophet's control; the bone prophet can give all these minions the same command with a single action that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. Any skeletal minions that still remain after 10 minutes crumble to dust.

**Serpentfolk Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 26 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d4 poison damage and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d4 poison damage and enfeebled 1 (1 round)

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-468" />
name: Bone Prophet

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The mysterious undead known as shadows lurk in dark places and feed on those who stray too far from the light.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Incorporeal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=632" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=722" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 306

**Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +14

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -5

**Dex** +4

**Con** +0

**Int** -2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +3
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 20 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +14 

**Will** +12 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 40
([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83))

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95)

**Resistances**
all 5 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))

**Weaknesses**
light vulnerability

**Light Vulnerability** Attacks against the shadow are treated as [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) if made by a creature who is in magical light or with an object that is in magical light (such as from the [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) spell).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shadow hand +15 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d6+3 void

**Shadow Spawn**   When a creature's shadow is pulled free by Steal Shadow, it becomes a shadow spawn under the command of the shadow that created it. This shadow spawn doesn't have Steal Shadow and is perpetually and incurably [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61). If the creature the shadow spawn was pulled from dies, the shadow spawn becomes a full-fledged, autonomous shadow. If the creature recovers from its enfeeblement, its shadow returns to it and the shadow spawn is extinguished.

**Slink in Shadows**   The shadow can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or end its [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) in a creature's or object's shadow.

**Steal Shadow** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Requirements** The shadow hit a living creature with a shadow hand Strike on its previous action; **Effect** The shadow pulls at the target's shadow, making the creature [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71). This is cumulative with other enfeebled conditions from shadows, to a maximum of enfeebled 4. If this increases a creature's enfeebled value to 3 or more, the target's shadow is separated from its body (see shadow spawn). The enfeebled value from Steal Shadow decreases by 1 every hour.

</column>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

An apex predator among the coastal surface waters where it hunts, the great white shark is one of the largest shark species. These silent killers glide gracefully through the ocean, always in search of their next meal.
