markdown:
Shining children are wicked, enigmatic monsters that roam remote planes and untraveled corners of the universe in search of esoteric lore. With their abnormally gaunt frames, long white hair, and unnerving, four-fingered hands, shining children are both strangely familiar and otherworldly in appearance, though they are barely visible within the shroud of blinding light they continually emit. Their faces are truly horrible, however, as their overlarge eyes and distended, gaping mouths reveal their heads to be voids filled with unnatural light. Shining children use this light as a weapon, weaving illusions and focusing beams of fiery brilliance. Because of their reputation as scholars of the alien and the eldritch, shining children are sometimes summoned by powerful wizards or occultists in search of rare knowledge. The creatures never give away their lore without some price, though, and typically demand in return the performance of contemptible deeds that further their inscrutable, far-ranging plans. These mysterious beings are natives of the [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=33). In that vast plane, accretions of metaphysical matter gradually accumulate and eventually coalesce into demiplanes. During this tumultuous process, sparks of living light sometimes shear off the newly formed planes, and these sparks of raw planar energy somehow transform into shining children. Every newly formed demiplane leaves a different mental imprint on its shining “offspring,” making it easy for shining children to telepathically recognize their brothers and sisters. Forever trapped in apparently adolescent bodies, shining children dedicate themselves to scholarship and violence with equal measure in a futile effort to understand their roles in the multiverse and the burning injustice of their births. Shining children confuse most other creatures with their refusal to use individual names in favor of alternating between referring to each other singularly and collectively. **[Recall Knowledge - Astral](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Shining Child](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3190) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 308 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 120 feet **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +18, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +23, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +21, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +18 **Str** +2 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +7 **Radiance Dependence** The shining child is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) while in areas of [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=409). --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +19 **Will** +19 **HP** 215 **Immunities** [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59), [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) **Blinding Aura** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640)) 60 feet. The shining child sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407). Any creature that starts its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 29 Fortitude save. If it fails, it is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 minute, and if it critically fails, it's permanently blinded. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune to this effect for 24 hours.
**Overwhelming Light** ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640)) **Trigger** The shining child enters an area of magical [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569) or begins its turn in an area of magical darkness; **Effect** The shining child attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the magical darkness (counteract rank 7, counteract modifier +23). --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 50 feet **Melee** fist +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d4+5 bludgeoning plus 4d6 fire and 2d4 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Melee** fire ray +25 ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d10+3 fire, plus 2d10 vitality damage if the target is [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 33 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) - **2nd** [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569) (at will) - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **5th** [False Vision](/Spells.aspx?ID=1522), [Mirage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) - **6th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Vibrant Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=1741), [Wall of Force](/Spells.aspx?ID=1749) - **7th** [Sunburst](/Spells.aspx?ID=1707) (x2)
Ancient Guardians The practice of conjuring and binding shining children to serve as guardians of important locations was a popular one in Thassilon. Even today, thousands of years after this empire's fall, adventurers can still encounter shining children in ancient ruins, guarding treasures and forgotten lore from the distant past.
name: Shining Child

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Shuln](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3191)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Scourges of the upper Darklands, these enormous, mole-like monstrosities slice and burrow through solid stone with massive forearms and adamantine-strong claws. Shulns grow to about 20 feet long and have four tiny, nearly imperceptible eyes; a long, pale snout; four thick-muscled legs that end in long, serrated claws; and a stubby pink tail. As a young shuln matures, its unique metabolism produces adamantine that becomes infused throughout its skeletal system. In addition to making their claws and fangs nearly unbreakable, this unique physiological trait makes shulns unparalleled burrowers and highly sought by monster hunters who hope to harvest the precious material from their corpses.

 Shulns have a ravenous appetite and eat nearly anything they can catch, but their preferred diet consists almost entirely of large invertebrates, especially [cave worms](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2871) (page 54). They rely on tiny sensory whiskers that cover their snouts and allow them to detect subtle movements in the air and ground without the use of vision. When they detect suitable prey, shulns bite their target at the first opportunity, injecting it with a potent paralytic toxin present in their saliva. So strong is this poison that it's capable of subduing even the near-unstoppable cave worm, making shulns a valuable (if dangerous) companion to anyone making excursions into worm-infested regions of the Darklands. Shulns' notoriously ill-tempered dispositions and their knack for digging into areas of an underground settlement where digging ought not to occur make them frustrating creatures to keep around, but when the alternative is an unpredictable but deadly visit from an enormous, hungry cave worm, the annoyances are well worth the trouble.

 Encounters with much larger shuln-like entities on the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=30) suggest that these creatures may have originated from there. The larger shulns still retain their elemental qualities, are quite a bit smarter, and have their own suite of earth-themed innate primal spells—but for all that, they still love the taste of cave worm.

<column gap="tiny">

([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 32

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27
</column>

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />

**Damage** 3d8+10 slashing

**Armor-Rending Strikes**   Any time the shuln scores a critical hit with a melee Strike, it also deals the same amount of damage to the target's armor, bypassing any Hardness lower than 10, as if [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374).

**Shuln Saliva**  ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 32 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison damage and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d6 poison damage and slowed 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 4d6 poison damage and [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) for 2d6 hours. Shuln saliva overcomes the inexorable ability.

**Unstoppable Burrow**   Shulns can burrow into solid rock and any metal with a hardness less than that of [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) as though it were soil or loose rubble, leaving a tunnel 10 feet in diameter.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Stubbornness and Spit</title>

Adventurers who fear encounters with [cave worms](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2871) might seek out a shuln and attempt to lure it along or magically compel it to aid in the fight to come, but the shuln's stubborn personality makes such efforts difficult and unreliable. More often, acquiring the shuln's saliva is an easier tactic, although the poison must be alchemically preserved if it is to be used as an injury poison, for this foul-smelling liquid breaks down quickly once it drools from the shuln's toothy maw.
</aside>

markdown:
Swirling down from misty peaks and through howling mountain passes like an evil wind, the vortex of bones known as a skulltaker is a terrible manifestation of the delirium and agony experienced by doomed climbers and lost trailblazers just before they met their end. In some places, a skulltaker is also known as a saxra. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Skulltaker](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3198) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 314 **Perception** +33; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); Skeletal Lore languages **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +34, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +35, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +30, [Skeletal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +32 **Str** +8 **Dex** +6 **Con** +6 **Int** +2 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +7 **Skeletal Lore** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A skulltaker taps into the memories of the creatures whose bones make up its body. This gives it the Skeletal Lore skill, which it can use to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) of any kind. In addition, it can speak and understand all the languages known by the creatures whose bones make up its body (typically including [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) and the regional language of the skulltaker's home region). The skulltaker can use Skeletal Lore as the primary skill check for the [_collective memories_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=113) ritual, and it can cast _collective memories_ without secondary casters. --- **AC** 42 **Fort** +31 **Ref** +33 **Will** +35 +1 status to all saves vs. [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) **HP** 300 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** bleed, [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** piercing 15, slashing 15 **Shard Storm** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) 10 feet. A cloud of bone shards surrounds the skulltaker. When a creature moves into the emanation or begins its turn there, shard storm deals 4d6 slashing damage and 4d6 void damage to the creature, with a DC 40 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.
If the creature has resistance or immunity to void damage, or an effect that protects it against [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, or an effect that protects it against the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) or [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) condition, the creature must first succeed at a DC 40 Will save or have all such benefits suppressed for 1 minute. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** jaws +35 ([deadly 2d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+14 piercing plus 3d6 void and vitality drain **Melee** claw +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [deadly 2d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d6+14 slashing plus 3d6 void and vitality drain **Ranged** bone javelin +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 3d8+6 piercing plus 3d6 void **Divine Innate Spells** DC 40 - **8th** [Desiccate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1484) (×2), [Execute](/Spells.aspx?ID=1519) (×2), [Punishing Winds](/Spells.aspx?ID=1643) (×2) - **Constant (6th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Rituals** DC 40 - **7th** [Collective Memories](/Rituals.aspx?ID=113) (see Skeletal Lore) **Bonetaker** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) Whenever a creature dies within 60 feet of a skulltaker, the skulltaker draws a portion of the creature's bones into its shard storm. The creature must succeed at a DC 40 Will save or rise as a [skeletal champion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3194) in 1d4 rounds. These skeletal champions are controlled by the skulltaker. **Splintered Ground** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The skulltaker causes splintered bones to erupt from all solid surfaces in a 100-foot emanation, except for surfaces of worked stone. A creature moving through the bones takes 10 piercing damage and 10 void damage for every 5 feet of movement. The first time each round a creature takes piercing damage from these splintered bones, it must succeed at a DC 40 Reflex save or take a –10-foot circumstance penalty to all Speeds for 10 minutes, or a –15-foot circumstance penalty for 24 hours on a critical failure. The bones remain in place until the skulltaker uses this action again or the bones are manually removed, which takes 10 minutes for each 5-foot square. **Vitality Drain** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) When a skulltaker hits with a melee Strike, the target must succeed at a DC 40 Fortitude save or become [drained 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) and [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67).
Skulltaker Insight The cooperation of a skulltaker is a powerful asset, for this whirling mass of death retains the collective memories of the creatures whose bones form its body. Because mountain travelers come from far and wide, a skulltaker's knowledge is often vast, spanning a range of topics.
name: Skulltaker

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Slurk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3199)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The slurk is a sticky, tusked frog-beast found in underground lairs and caves. It has two massive tusks that it uses to gore prey and tangle with rival slurks. With the slurk's natural ability to climb walls and cling effortlessly to ceilings, it can be easy for unwary cave explorers to end up on the wrong end of these formidable ivory tusks.

 Slurks exude two very different types of foul-smelling secretions from their pale white skin. Large pustules on the slurk's back drip a sticky resin-like slime that quickly hardens upon exposure to air. By flexing its skin, the slurk can burst these pustules in the direction of intruders, covering its foes in sticky goo and severely limiting their ability to withstand the monster's other attacks, including the effects of its other secretion. Glands along the slurk's ventral side excrete an incredibly slippery and fetid grease, which protects the slurk from the immobilizing effects of its own back slime but also has the added benefit of making it extremely difficult to grapple and capture. The best way to discover if a slurk is in the vicinity is to look for hard clumps of such grease, which accumulate and dry in cave corners and amid rock piles where slurks rest between meals.

 Slurks are thought to be descendants of a failed dwarven attempt to domesticate and breed large subterranean frogs as food and labor animals. Despite this apparent failure, others who live underground often befriend slurks. The sticky frog-beasts have proven extremely desirable to [kobolds](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=435), who now domesticate and train slurks as powerful mounts and guardians. While other creatures, particularly [boggards](/Languages.aspx?ID=27), sometimes train slurks to serve as guardians, kobolds remain those who use these creatures the most. A kobold mounted on a slurk will often hide in the upper ledges of a cave, using the advantages of height and surprise to harry foes with ranged attacks. Kobold riders also take advantage of the slurk's ability to climb, and charge at their enemies from the walls of a cavern.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Slurk.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 2">[Slurk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3199)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 315

**Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +6, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +5

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +2

**Con** +4

**Int** -4

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +0
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +10 (+12 vs. [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376))

**Ref** +6 

**Will** +4 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 35

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tusks +11 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570)),
**Damage** 1d8+4 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
slime squirt +9 ([range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** entangling slime

**Belly Grease** <actions string="Three Actions" />  The slurk extrudes a slippery grease from its ventral glands to coat the floor under it and in a 5-foot emanation, turning the affected area into uneven ground for 10 minutes, after which it dries to a putrid crust. The DC to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369) across the slime is 18.

**Entangling Slime**   A creature struck by a slurk's slime squirt becomes [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) and takes a –5-foot penalty to Speed for 1 hour or until the slime is removed. The slime can be removed with a total of three Interact actions by the entangled creature or creatures adjacent to the creature. These actions don't need to be consecutive or made by the same creature.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Slurk Riding</title>

As long as a slurk is willing or broken, a creature at least one size smaller than the slurk can use it as a mount. A slurk's back slime grants its rider a +2 circumstance bonus against any attempts to physically dismount the rider.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Giant Anaconda](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3203)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The monstrous giant anaconda is a constrictor snake capable of swallowing whole creatures as big as horses—to say nothing of their riders. Although novice bushwhackers watch out for snakes that might drop on them from the jungle canopy above, giant anacondas are most commonly encountered in ponds and rivers, where they feed on prey including manatees and capybaras. This hunting tactic allows anacondas to drown their prey while constricting it, reducing the chance of injuries while hunting. Despite lurid and sensational stories spread by adventurers, these snakes rarely attack humanoids.

markdown:
Soulbound dolls are eerie mannequins or playthings that have been imbued with a small piece of a deceased mortal's soul. These little constructs are created for a variety of reasons—such as to serve as companions or servants— but their free will means their obedience to their creators is hardly a given. Followers of [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290) generally abhor soulbound dolls, viewing them as a perversion of the natural cycle of souls, and those who worship the Lady of Graves see the destruction of a soulbound doll, regardless of the construct's behavior, as an important service to the Great Beyond. Soulbound dolls are the simplest in a series of soulbound constructs, including human-sized soulbound mannequins, powerful soulbound shells, and sentinel soulbound terra-cotta warriors. Creating them from unwilling living creatures is cruel, and an unwilling donor can resist the process with a successful Will save against the creator's [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) DC, ruining the doll if not preventing the donor's death. A doll can also be crafted from the soul of a person who has given consent to such use before their death. Soulbound dolls encountered by adventurers are typically guardians of some sort; despite their diminutive size, the soul fragment's power makes the doll's fist more dangerous than a casual observer would expect. Further, it grants the doll a single spell of outsized power, given its stature. Because of their autonomy and remarkable intelligence, soulbound dolls are occasionally employed by their crafters as administrators over much more powerful but mindless constructs, allowing such dolls to control defenses far beyond their own capabilities. Though soulbound dolls contain a small fragment of a soul extracted during or shortly after a person's death, this doesn't affect the deceased's resurrection or progress to the afterlife. This extraction process is typically lethal to otherwise living prospective soul donors. The soul fragment resides in a soul focus gem (Hardness 10) typically embedded in the doll's neck or chest. The soul fragment generally clings to a strong personality trait of the original soul, but the doll continues to learn from its initial state, meaning its personality and abilities can change, possibly growing closer to the donor's or moving farther afield on its own individual path. The soulbound doll's focus gem retains the doll's memories even after the doll's destruction. The intact soul focus gem of a destroyed doll can even be placed into a new doll body by someone knowledgeable in the creation of soulbound creatures, effectively reconstituting the soulbound doll. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Soulbound Doll](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3204) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 318 **Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); one spoken by its creator (typically [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155)) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +8, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +4, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +8 **Str** -2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Personality Fragments** A soulbound doll shares fragments of its donor soul's personality, though none of that creature's memories. This causes a soulbound doll to match a strong personality trait of the donor soul (see sidebar). Because of its soul sliver, a soulbound doll is not immune to spirit as most constructs are. --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +7 **Ref** +10 **Will** +6 **HP** 23 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Resistances** bludgeoning 3, piercing 5, slashing 3 --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** fist +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 0 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d6+2 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 18, attack +10 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715) - **2nd** one spell based on donor soul's personality trait (see sidebar) - **3rd** [Levitate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1584)
Soulbound Personalities A soulbound doll's additional 2nd-rank innate spell depends on a strong personality trait it had in life, as listed below. **Brave**: [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1514)
**Calm**: [_calm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458)
**Careful**: [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445)
**Cruel**: [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552)
**Gentle**: [_peaceful rest_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1625)
**Impish**: [_disguise magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1491)
**Jolly**: [_laughing fit_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583)
**Kind**: [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554)
**Rash**: [_breathe fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1457)
**Sassy**: [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493)
**Timid**: [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577)
name: Soulbound Doll

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Sphinx](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3205)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Sphinxes are mystical beings with the body of a lion, the wings of a great bird, and the upper torso and head of a human. They are often maligned in legends as nothing more than monsters, and though they are quick to anger and are capable of exacting deadly retribution for perceived slights, they are also highly intelligent.

 Sphinxes are often associated with desert regions, but they can dwell in more moderate climates. They form small groups consisting of a single, extended family that hunts and works together to protect and teach their young. As they mature, sphinxes develop a drive to gather hidden lore and solve the world's greatest riddles—the trait that is perhaps most often identified with their kind.

 While sometimes bound into service as guardians for powerful spellcasters, lone sphinxes may also be encountered on journeys of discovery and as purveyors of esoteric lore. If treated with the proper respect—and fed well—a sphinx can demonstrate a willingness to exchange information. Their favorite currency is, of course, riddles and secrets. One who can trade knowledge for knowledge has a much better chance of succeeding while bargaining with a sphinx. However, a sphinx's insatiable thirst for new riddles, as well as their extensive collection of secrets accumulated over hundreds of years of life, makes it difficult to offer them something they don't already know. Those who attempt to trade petty insight and stale riddles may invoke a sphinx's ire and will not live long enough to regret it.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 24

([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Sphinx](/Languages.aspx?ID=35); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Bardic Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +19, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +16, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +18, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +17

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +1

**Con** +3

**Int** +5

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Bardic Lore** Sphinxes are naturally curious, and their love of puzzles and mysteries leads them to gather information on a broad range of topics. Sphinxes have Bardic Lore, a special [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) skill that can be used only to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367), but on any topic.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 27 

**Fort** +16 

**Ref** +14 

**Will** +19 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 135

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 35 feet, fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 2d6+9 slashing

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 27
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485)
- **2nd**
[See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663)
- **3rd**
[Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1588)
- **4th**
[Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465) (at will), [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466) (at will), [Cleanse Affliction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467), [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647)
- **Constant (5th)**
[Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Claw Rake** <actions string="Three Actions" />  The sphinx rears back on their hind legs and makes two claw Strikes at the same target, using the same attack bonus as their highest melee attack. If both attacks deal damage, the target takes extra damage equal to one claw Strike.

**Pounce** <actions string="Single Action" />  The sphinx Strides and makes a Strike at the end of that movement. If the sphinx began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), they remain hidden until after the attack.

**Riddler's Rune**   Once per week, a Sphinx can create a magical symbol as the [_rune trap_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=124) ritual. The sphinx usually shapes it to take the form of a written riddle and sets the password to the answer. A creature that gives the wrong answer or tries to pass without answering must succeed at a DC 26 Will save or be affected by one of the following spells, chosen by the sphinx when creating the symbol: [_synaptic pulse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1710) (5th), [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) (4th), [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) (4th), [_phantom pain_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632) (4th), [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675) (4th). The sphinx learns the identity of any creature that answers the riddle and tends to be friendly to them if they answered correctly.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Sphinx Riddles</title>

Sphinxes are well known for their love of riddles, a love that often moves into the realm of obsession. A would-be foe who can answer a sphinx's favorite riddles—or better yet, can stump the sphinx with a crafty and creative riddle of their own—can often avoid combat with the creature and even secure their aid.
</aside>

It can hang from the web or drop off. The distance it Descends on a Web doesn't count for [falling damage](/Rules.aspx?ID=402). A creature that successfully Strikes the web (AC 20, Hardness 3, 5 HP) severs it, causing the spider to fall.

**Hunting Spider Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 16 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d4 poison damage and [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d6 poison damage, [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), and off-guard (1 round); **Stage 3** 1d6 poison damage, clumsy 2, and off-guard (1 round)

**Web Trap**   A creature hit by the hunting spider's web Strike is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) and stuck to the nearest surface until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 17).

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Goliath Spider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3209)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Goliath spiders dwell in the deepest jungles, where they build webs as big as temples and feast on prey as large as hippopotamuses.

<column gap="tiny">

**Cha** -4

**Will** +17

</row>

**Spring Upon Prey** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Requirements** Initiative has not yet been rolled; **Trigger** A creature touches the goliath spider's web while the spider is on it; **Effect** The goliath spider automatically notices the creature and Strides, Climbs, or Descends on a Web before it rolls initiative.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 45 feet, climb 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fangs +24 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d12+12 piercing plus goliath spider venom

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
web +22 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** web tether

**Descend on a Web** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) The goliath spider moves straight down up to 120 feet, suspended by a web line. It can hang from the web or drop off. The distance it Descends on a Web doesn't count for [falling damage](/Rules.aspx?ID=402). A creature that successfully Strikes the web (AC 20, Hardness 5, 20 HP) severs it, causing the spider to fall.

**Goliath Spider Venom**  ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 30 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d6 poison damage and **slowed 1** (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d8 poison damage and slowed 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 3d10 poison damage and [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) for 2d4 hours

**Web Tether**   A creature hit by the spider's web Strike is [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) and tethered to the spider, preventing it from being moved farther away from the spider. The spider can have one creature tethered at a time. The DC to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) or [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) the web is 30. The tether can be severed with a Strike (AC 20, Hardness 5, HP 20), but this doesn't free the restrained creature.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Sprite](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3210)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Common sprites, sometimes called firefly sprites, are primeval guardians that latch onto a person, place, or object and defend it for their own inscrutable reasons. Their dispositions vary from kind to spiteful, but all sprites have a capricious streak. Being only about 9 inches tall, they are wary of animals that might hunt them, particularly house cats, and prefer flight to a fight. On the other hand, sprites are incredibly curious about all forms of magic and heedlessly gather around ley line nexuses or other places of power.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8
</column>

</row>

**Luminous Fire** ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) A sprite naturally sheds light like a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2760). The sprite can extinguish, rekindle, or change the color of this light by using an action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. While this light is extinguished, the sprite's Strikes don't deal fire damage, and they can't use their luminous spark Strike.

The mischievous dragon sprites called draxies have dueled their pixie cousins for the title of ultimate prankster for centuries. They exercise patience and planning to create the perfect pranks, spending months, or even years, on their efforts. One exception to their flighty nature is the elucrea, a lifelong bond between a draxie and a creature they're particularly fond of, typically one with a good sense of humor. According to draxie legend, a little piece of a draxie's spirit remembers being united as the ancient [fey dragonet](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2957) Elucredassa, and that causes draxies to yearn for such connections with others. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Draxie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3211) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 322 **Perception** +8; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) (touch) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +9, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +10, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +8, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11 **Str** -1 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +11 **Will** +8 **HP** 45 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5 --- **Speed** 15 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** jaws +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 0 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d8+3 piercing **Ranged** euphoric spark +7 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d4+3 mental **Primal Innate Spells** DC 20 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639) - **1st** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) (×3) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577), [Revealing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653) **Draxie Dust** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The draxie breathes magical dust in a 15-foot cone. Roll 1d4 to determine the effect. Each creature in the area must succeed at a DC 17 Will save or be affected. The draxie can't use Draxie Dust again for 1d4 rounds.
  1. The target takes the effects of the [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) spell.
  2. The target loses its last 5 minutes of memory.
  3. The target takes the effects of a [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675) spell.
  4. For 1 minute, the target is in a state of euphoria that makes it [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92).
name: Draxie

Insatiably curious, overly excitable, and just a bit puckish, pixies are wanderers and tricksters who use their pixie dust to create all sorts of whimsical situations, as well as to defend themselves. They take great pride in their skill at archery, even when not using their arrows to deliver magical ailments. They often practice by shooting drops of dew or severing vines and hairs. Other creatures often have trouble understanding a pixie's rapid, rambling way of speaking. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Pixie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3212) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 323 **Perception** +12; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +11, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11 **Str** -1 **Dex** +5 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 **Items** [Longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=76) (60 arrows), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43) --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +14 **Will** +12 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 40 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5 --- **Speed** 15 feet, fly 45 feet **Melee** shortsword +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6+4 piercing **Ranged** longbow +13 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 1d8+4 piercing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 21 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671) - **1st** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) - **2nd** [Entangling Flora](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515), [Revealing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653) - **3rd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) - **4th** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only) **Sprinkle Pixie Dust** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The pixie sprinkles pixie dust onto one of their arrows. If the pixie hits a creature with that arrow before the pixie's next turn, the arrow inflicts one of the following special effects of the pixie's choice instead of dealing damage. Each effect depends on the target's DC 21 Will save. On a critical hit, the target gets a result one degree worse than it rolled.
  • Charm ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The arrow has the effect of a charm spell, except the target doesn't gain a bonus to its save if the only hostile act was the pixie firing its bow, and the pixie can choose to direct the target's adoration toward another creature rather than itself.
  • Memory Loss ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) On a failed Will save, the target loses the last 5 minutes of its memory.
  • Sleep ([incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)) The target suffers the effects of a 3rd- rank sleep spell.
  • Subdual ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661)) The target takes 4d6 mental damage, depending on its basic Will save.
Variant Pixie Arrows Pixies can enchant their arrows with a wide variety of effects, though nearly all such enchantments are designed to charm or bewilder, never maim. Some arrows may emulate the effects of spells such as [_confusion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [_laughing fit_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583), and even [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693).
name: Pixie

Stone bulwarks are slow and steady constructs typically carved from marble or granite. They're often made to serve as works of art when at rest, so some magical crafters employ master sculptors to ensure their constructs make beautiful statues. Older stone bulwarks might be weathered, with scuffed or cracked surfaces or missing noses and digits, but this weathering is largely cosmetic and doesn't adversely impact the bulwark's functionality. Bulwark Variants Immense stone bulwarks reside in some ancient ruins, survivors from time-lost civilizations bent on carrying out orders from long-gone masters. These bulwarks are level 15 or higher, and most are Gargantuan. Because their size is so great and the structures they dwell in so dilapidated, the awakening of such stone bulwarks can cause surrounding structures to collapse, ancient foundations to buckle, and ceilings to come crashing down. They can attack with wide, sweeping strikes capable of knocking down multiple targets at once. Serpentstone bulwarks are snake- or hydra-shaped variants first created by [serpentfolk](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=468) artisans. They have Serpentstone Breath, but lack binding stone and Inexorable March.
**Serpentstone Breath** ([earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The bulwark breathes a 60- foot cone of green gas. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 34 Fortitude save. The bulwark can't use Serpentstone Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature's body hardens, causing it to become [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature becomes [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87) for 1 minute. It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns.
**Critical Failure** The creature becomes petrified permanently. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Stone Bulwark](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3213) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 324 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +26 **Str** +7 **Dex** -1 **Con** +4 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +18 **Will** +19 **HP** 175 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374)), spells 10 (except [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) **Statuary Aura** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585)) 20 feet. Rocks of marble magically arise from the ground in the aura. They protect the bulwark's allies, giving each of them [standard cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459). These stones can be used for Throw Rock. This aura automatically activates at the start of the stone bulwark's first turn in combat and deactivates at the end of combat. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** fist +24 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+13 bludgeoning and binding stone **Ranged** rock +22 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d6+11 bludgeoning and binding stone **Binding Stone** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585)) Any creature hit by the stone bulwark's fist or rock Strike is affected by a DC 30 [_earthbind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506) spell. **Inexorable March** The stone bulwark Strides up to its Speed, pushing back each creature whose space it moves into and damaging them if they try to stop its movement. A creature can attempt to bar the way by succeeding at a DC 34 Fortitude save. On a critical success, the resisting creature takes no damage; otherwise it is damaged as if hit by the construct's fist. **[Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=80)**
Stone Slabs Depending on the material from which it is made and the care that went into crafting it, a destroyed stone bulwark may be worth as much as an immaculately sculpted marble pillar or as little as a pile of rubble.
name: Stone Bulwark

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Tengu Sneak](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3214)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Tengu are an adaptable people originally from the continent of Tian Xia, but whose travels have taken them across all of Golarion. As a people in diaspora, tengu are almost always found within larger kingdoms and communities of other peoples, with the exception of their home nation of Kwanlai. As a result, tengu tend to gather in close-knit social groups both with other tengu as well as with other peoples of non-majority ancestries, collecting words and customs from other cultures the way a bird collects trinkets for its nest.

 Although humanoid, tengu have very distinct, birdlike features, and many would say that they resemble crows more than they do humans. They have strong, thick beaks, as well as sharp talons at the ends of their arms and legs. Most of a tengu's body is covered in small feathers that range in color from dark brown and midnight blue to glossy black, with lighter colors being rare but not unheard of. Like many avian creatures, tengu have hollow bones, making them much lighter than other humanoids of their size, and some tengu even possess wings that allow them to fly.

 Tengu have a strong cultural focus on the sky, considering tall mountaintops sacred places and worshipping gods associated with nature and storms, like Gozreh or Hei Feng. They have a long and proud tradition of both martial arts and smithing, and many aspiring Tian heroes have sought out a tengu mentor or swordsmith. Tengu magic revolves around using tengu feathers, bound into a fan, as a medium to command wind and lightning, and some tengu even have the power to “eat” misfortune—skills that only help tengu as they continue expanding across Golarion to new lands.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Terotricus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3215)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The legendary terotricus is a massive slime mold that hails from the [Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45). Its collective consciousness encapsulates entire regions, spreading as far as its ever-growing cloud of spores will take it. Once it has seeped into [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) from the Rifts, a terotricus's agenda is to feed on all living creatures, infecting them with its spores, and its presence can spell doom for any in its way.

 Terotricuses move by rapidly expanding and contracting their slimy “bodies,” which are capable of burrowing through soil, gliding across water, and scrabbling up steep slopes. These behemoths of rot don't need to travel to see their plans come to fruition, though; their spores easily latch onto demons and other denizens of the Outer Rifts, who in turn bring this blight to the Universe when the fiends are summoned.

 When a terotricus infects a creature with its spores, web-like fungal growths start appearing on the victim's skin until they cover the entire body, at which point the victim's mind is also subdued and bent to the terotricus's will. The terotricus's favored victims include animals, elves, and fey, though it is happy to infect any creature it can catch. Cults of [Treerazer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=394) occasionally attempt to make contact with a terotricus, but such acts almost always simply result in a new sect of spore-blighted servants.

**Damage** 4d10+18 bludgeoning plus 2d6 spirit and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) or [Improved Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) 20 feet

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
spores +37 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 80 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 4d8+8 poison plus 2d6 spirit, spore blight, and sticky spores

**Infest Environs** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** The terotricus is in a swamp or forested area; **Effect** The terotricus drains nutrients from nearby trees and undergrowth while simultaneously infesting them with fungal growth. All non-magical plant life (though not [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) creatures) within a 60-foot emanation withers and sprouts foul mold and slimy mushrooms, removing any [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) and [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) provided by trees and undergrowth. In addition, the terotricus regains 200 Hit Points (this is a [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) effect).

**Spore Blight**  ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) [Plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) and [fungi](/Traits.aspx?ID=614) are immune; **Saving Throw** DC 40 Fortitude; **Stage 1** [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 day); **Stage 2** enfeebled 4 and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) (1 day); **Stage 3** [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) by the terotricus (as [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501); 5d8 days); **Stage 4** dead

**Sticky Spores**   A creature hit by a terotricus's spores takes a –10-foot status penalty to all its Speeds for 1 minute. If the Strike was a critical hit, the creature is also [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 40).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Terotricus Myths</title>

The Kellids of Sarkoris dealt with their fair share of terotricuses during the era of the Worldwound, and these people developed unique rituals to purify tainted grounds with the help of ancestral spirits and feathers acquired from [celestials](/Traits.aspx?ID=551). Far south of there, in what is now known as the Sodden Lands, wastelanders who learn of the presence of a terotricus—or “swampblight,” as they're called there— carry lanterns blessed by angels in the hopes that these lights will keep the terotricus at bay.
</aside>
name: Terotricus

markdown:
Treerazer, the self-styled Lord of the Blasted Tarn, is a powerful demon on the cusp of ascending to the true power of one of the rulers of the Outer Rifts itself—a demon lord. For now, even as a nascent demon lord, Treerazer is a dangerous foe. Treerazer rarely leaves his swampy realm of Tanglebriar—a large thicket of tainted foliage and rotting detritus just south of Kyonin's Fierani Forest— but can be encountered anywhere within that toxic mire, often accompanied by a small legion of demons, corrupted fey, and other deadly allies. Certain occult rituals have the power to call him forth from Tanglebriar, granting him the opportunity to directly work his evils beyond the realm to which he has been exiled. Some believe that no eldritch force contains Treerazer and that, were he willing, he could travel Golarion with impunity, spreading the twisted blessings of his touch and the corruption of his presence, yet the Lord of the Blasted Tarn is as cunning and canny as he is deadly, and prefers to work his evils on the world from the safety of his nightmare realm. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 60 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 58 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 55 [Treerazer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3218) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 328 **Perception** +46; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 300 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +40, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +38, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +45, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +46, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +49, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +38, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +45, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +40 **Str** +12 **Dex** +9 **Con** +11 **Int** +7 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +8 **Items** [_Blackaxe_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=490) --- **AC** 54 **Fort** +42 **Ref** +40 **Will** +43 **HP** 550 , regeneration 50 (deactivated by [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) **Resistances** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) 20, [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 15, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 15, physical 20 (except [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375)) **Weaknesses** holy 20 **Aura of Corruption** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) 120 feet. Plants near Treerazer twist, deform, and transform into thorny or fungoid parodies of their natural shapes. A living creature in this area must succeed at a DC 47 Fortitude save each round or become partially transformed into plantlike matter. Those who fail this saving throw are treated as if they were [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) for the purposes of any effect that particularly harms or inconveniences plant creatures more than other creatures, but do not gain any benefits of being plant creatures. This effect lasts as long as the creature remains within the area of corruption and for 1 minute thereafter.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 60 feet, swim 40 feet; [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** _Blackaxe_ +47 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 5d12+18 slashing plus 1d6 acid (plus an additional 2d6 slashing to plants) **Melee** jaws +45 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 5d10+20 slashing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 49, attack +43 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) - **2nd** [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=1717) (at will) - **3rd** [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506) (at will) - **4th** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) - **6th** [Tangling Creepers](/Spells.aspx?ID=1714) (at will) - **9th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (at will) - **10th** [Desiccate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1484), [Freeze Time](/Spells.aspx?ID=1538), [Wall of Thorns](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752) - **Constant (8th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Rituals** DC 49 - **1st** [Demonic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=142) - **5th** [Planar Servitor](/Rituals.aspx?ID=120) **Defoliation** ([Plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) Treerazer exudes a pulse of sickly green light in a 30-foot-radius emanation. All plants in the area (including creatures under the effect of his aura of corruption) blacken and wither. Non-creature plants immediately wither and die. [Plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) and [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614) creatures take 20d8 void damage with a DC 49 basic Fortitude save. A creature that fails its save is [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) for 1 minute and [sickened 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91). Treerazer can choose to exclude any number of plants or fungi in the area from this effect, and generally does so to preserve twisted and corrupted plants or fungi, or plant and fungus creatures that are allied to his cause. Treerazer can't use Defoliation for 1d4 rounds. **Dispelling Strike** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per round; **Trigger** Treerazer hits a creature, object, or spell effect with a weapon Strike or subjects one to Defoliation; **Effect** Treerazer casts his innate [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), targeting the creature he hit with his Strike or one spell affecting that creature. **Staggering Strike** When Treerazer scores a critical hit with a melee attack, the target is [stunned 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93).
Spawn Of Cyth-V'sug Treerazer was once the favored minion and lieutenant (some even say child) of the demon lord [Cyth-V'sug](/Deities.aspx?ID=74), Lord of Fungus and Parasites. After a failed attempt to wrest that role in the [Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45) away from Cyth-V'sug, Treerazer fled to the mortal [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). Cyth-V'sug was unable (or perhaps only unwilling) to pursue but took steps to ensure that Treerazer would remain there by exiling him, severing Treerazer's bond to the Outer Rifts—if the Lord of the Blasted Tarn is slain, his animus will not return to the Outer Rifts and reform. Death, to Treerazer, is a permanent thing.
Treerazer's Cultists Treerazer is worshipped by cultists throughout the Inner Sea region. The majority of these can be found within or near the expanse of the Tanglebriar, consisting of fungus-corrupted fey, debased elves, or other sinister demon worshippers. Beyond Tanglebriar, his cultists are rarer and tend to be loners or leaders of very small groups. Treerazer's religious symbol is a bleeding dead tree that's been split in half. **Areas of Concern** corruption of nature, pollution, and slaughter of elves
**Edicts** corrupt plant life with evil or fungal influences, slay elves, feast on rotten flesh or fungus
**Anathema** grant mercy to elves, plant trees, encourage natural plant growth
**Divine Attribute** Strength or Wisdom Devotee Benefits **Cleric Spells** 1st: [_grim tendrils_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1548), 3rd: [_wall of thorns_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752), 6th: [_tangling creepers_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1714)
**Divine Font** [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) or [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554)
**Divine Sanctification** must choose unholy
**Divine Skill** [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)
**Domains** [destruction](/Domains.aspx?ID=71), [nature](/Domains.aspx?ID=85), [nightmares](/Domains.aspx?ID=86), [tyranny](/Domains.aspx?ID=96)
**Favored Weapon** [greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=376)
name: Treerazer

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Forest Troll](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3219)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Forest trolls are gangly giants who stalk the fringes of civilization. They rely on their incredible strength to overpower foes with their vicious claws and toothy maws. These trolls stand anywhere from 12 to 16 feet tall, though they prefer to hunch for comfort and to lull foes into a false sense of security. Trolls are truly creatures of the forest, deeply linked in flesh and blood to the verdant habitats they consider their territory. In fact, when a forest troll is killed, their flesh turns into blackened lumps of charcoal, often still glowing with coals. Despite that, these creatures roam farther and wider than most of their kin, to the extent that they're the first creatures a common person thinks of when they hear the word “troll.”

markdown:
Long-lived trolls sometimes force their way through the twin distractions of hunger and pain to learn more about the world around them. To manage their regeneration, they learn to cover themselves in crude armor that slowly becomes integrated with the top layer of their flesh. Marked by this armor and aided by their superior wits, these warleaders gather a variety of trolls to serve them in raiding parties. The warleader presented here is a forest troll, but warleaders can be other types of trolls as well. Many of them are adapted to battle tactics that are best suited to their home environments. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Troll Warleader](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3220) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 331 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +22, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +17, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +17 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +7 **Int** -1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +4 **Easily Misled** The troll warleader gets a –4 circumstance penalty to their Perception DC against [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks. **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [battle axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=371)_ (2), [Half Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=12) --- **AC** 29 (26 plus [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) after Shed Armor) **Fort** +23 **Ref** +17 **Will** +15 **HP** 240 , regeneration 20 (deactivated by [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Weaknesses** electricity 10, fire 10 **Furious Roar** **Trigger** The troll warleader takes electricity or fire damage; **Effect** The warleader uses their Primordial Roar and, if they're aware of the damage's source, can Stride toward it. If the warleader has [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), they attempt a DC 15 flat check to remove it.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** jaws +23 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+13 piercing **Melee** battle axe +24 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 2d8+13 slashing **Melee** claw +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+13 slashing **Primordial Roar** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The troll warleader unleashes a bestial roar. Each non-troll creature in a 100-foot emanation must attempt a DC 29 Will save. The creature is then temporarily immune for 10 minutes.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76).
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 3. **Shed Armor** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The warleader cuts their armor loose from their flesh. They immediately heal 60 Hit Points in a surge of regeneration as they grow twisted limbs and malformed faces. Without their armor, the warleader's AC drops to 26 but they gain [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) from the new faces. Putting the armor back on takes 10 minutes, and this ability can't be used again until 1 hour has passed. **Sweeping Axes** **Requirements** The troll warleader is wielding two battle axes; **Effect** The warleader makes a battle axe Strike against each creature in their reach and the bonus from [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708) applies to each attack. These attacks count against their multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks.
name: Troll Warleader

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Unicorn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3223)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Symbols of grace and purity, unicorns resemble proud and noble horses. They typically have pure white coats, but they are best known for the single, delicate horn that extends from the center of their forehead. While unicorns often serve as protectors of unspoiled wilderness and sacred places, they are themselves highly sought after for their horns, which are said to possess potent magical properties. To many, the mere idea of hunting such a magnificent creature in the hopes of severing its horn is utterly reprehensible. Indeed, a dehorned unicorn is a sorry sight, and few such unicorns survive much longer in the wild.

 Unicorns are found almost exclusively in remote, unsullied areas of wilderness. Sometimes associated with good-aligned deities, other times associated with nature and the fey, unicorns are always known for their righteousness and nobility. They are wary, at best, of most humanoid creatures, due in large part to poachers' tendency to hunt them for their horns, but unicorns are often rumored to have a weakness for those who are pure of both heart and spirit. Despite some tales, unicorns are equally likely to recognize purity in people of all genders.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18

**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Unicorn.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=547" />

</traits>

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 333

**Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +10, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +8, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +9

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +3

**Con** +3

**Int** +0

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Animal Empathy** The unicorn has a connection to the creatures of the natural world that allows them to communicate with [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531). They can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) skill with animals.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 20 

**Fort** +10 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +11 (+2 vs. [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647))

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 45

</row>

**Immunities**
[poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 45 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
horn +12 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 1d10+4 piercing plus 1d4 spirit and ghost touch

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hoof +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 1d8+4 bludgeoning and ghost touch

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 21
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585)
- **3rd**
[Cleanse Affliction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) (×2)
- **5th**
[Nature's Pathway](/Spells.aspx?ID=1613)

**Ghost Touch**   A unicorn's Strikes have the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune.

**Powerful Charge** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The unicorn Strides up to double its Speed in a straight line and then makes a horn Strike. If the unicorn moved at least 20 feet, it deals an additional 2d6 damage on a hit.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Alicorn</title>

In alchemical and occult circles, “alicorn” is the word for the material that makes up a unicorn's horn, also used to describe any object made from that horn. Note that in many markets, alicorn sales are illegal, and those who attempt to sell alicorn are viewed with repugnance—or worse.
</aside>

Vampire masterminds are evil wizards who control entire cabals of the undead and instill fear in the hearts of mortals far and wide. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Vampire Mastermind](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3226) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 337 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); plus one regional language **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +20, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +22, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +19 **Str** +3 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +6 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 **Children of the Night** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Items** signet ring --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +18 **Will** +20 **HP** 115 (coffin restoration, [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 10, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920)) **Vampire Vulnerabilities**
**Mist Escape** --- **Speed** 25 feet, climb 25 feet **Melee** claw +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d8+9 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 29, attack +19 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=1673) - **1st** [Dizzying Colors](/Spells.aspx?ID=1500) (×2), [Enfeeble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513), [Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531) - **2nd** [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455), [Floating Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=1533), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577), [Stupefy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1691) - **3rd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [Lightning Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=1586) - **4th** [Fire Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1529), [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568), [Vision of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742), [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1748) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) Giant bat with fangs +19 for 2d8+9 piercing. **Create Servitor** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=580)) **Dominate** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) DC 26 **Drain Bonded Item** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** The vampire mastermind hasn't acted yet on this turn; **Effect** The vampire expends the power stored in their signet ring. This gives the vampire the ability to cast one prepared spell they had already previously cast today without spending a spell slot. The vampire must still Cast the Spell and meet the spell's other requirements. **Drink Blood** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) When Drinking Blood, the vampire mastermind regains 10 HP. **Steady Spellcasting** If another creature's reaction would disrupt the vampire mastermind's spellcasting action, the vampire attempts a DC 15 flat check. If the vampire succeeds, their action isn't disrupted. **Turn to Mist** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670))
name: Vampire Mastermind

markdown:
The most common and numerous vescavors are the raving swarms. If left alone and unchecked, many believe they would eventually consume themselves once everything else in the immediate vicinity was consumed. What is even more vexing than their hunger is their song. While it sounds like gibberish at first, once bitten, the song will drive people into chaos. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Vescavor Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3227) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 338 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13 **Str** -2 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** -3 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +15 **Will** +9 **HP** 60 **Immunities** [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), precision, [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=78) **Resistances** bludgeoning 5, piercing 5, slashing 2 **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5 --- **Speed** 20 feet, fly 40 feet **Devour All** The swarm eat away the very earth beneath their feet. The swarms Strides. All squares they occupy during their movement becomes [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). Any creatures they move through must succeed a DC 21 Reflex save or fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). **Maddening Gibbers** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) Each [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) creature in the swarm's space must attempt a DC 21 Will saving throw as the swarm yammers the endless chorus of the [Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45).
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Maddening Gibbers for 1 minute.
**Success** The target is unaffected and is immune to Maddening Gibbers for 1 round.
**Failure** The target becomes [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target becomes confused for 1 round and can't target [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) while confused in this way. **Ravenous Bites** Each enemy in the swarm's space takes 3d6 piercing damage (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). A creature that fails its save is also [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) for 1 round.
name: Vescavor Swarm

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Vescavor Queen](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3228)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A vescavor queen is a horrifying sight. Much like their swarms, they resemble an acrid green wasp, but their abdomens are abnormally bloated and covered in acid-leaking spikes; their mouths make up the majority of their face and are filled with large, gnarled teeth, each the size of a human finger. They only possess one pair of arms, each the length of their entire body but abnormally folded and kept close, and their five pairs of wings are unnaturally stacked upon each other in a way that would make flight impossible for any natural creature. These queens ensure that the swarms never end their dreadful march towards wherever food can be found. Not only will they gradually create vescavor swarms, but they can also cause existing swarms to double in number at a moment's notice. These strengthened swarms are also blessed with increased vigor and speed. Moreover, a queen's acidic spit is laced with pheromones that drive her swarms into a rage, which she uses to direct the swarms towards priority targets.

 Occasionally, a powerful fiend will capture a vescavor queen and use her to create swarms in a controlled environment. However, the practice was quickly abandoned. For all the damage these swarms might cause to a fiendish master's enemies, it is rarely worth the cost of keeping the queen and her endless swarms of children fed and under control. Even keeping the queen in one location can be costly and difficult, as it is almost impossible to find a cage that a queen cannot eat her way out of, given sufficient time.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Vescavor_Queen.webp" />
</column>

</row>

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +20, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +16

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="tiny">

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 1d10+13 piercing plus 1d10 acid

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 2d10+8 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Melee**

stinger +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 2d4 piercing plus 2d10 acid

**Ranged**
