spit +19 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d8 acid plus rage pheromones

**Chaotic Spawning** <actions string="Three Actions" />  The vescavor queen strengthens her swarms. All [vescavor swarms](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3227) within 100 feet become Huge and [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 minute. Vescavor swarms can only use the extra action each round for the Ravenous Bites action.

**Feeding Time** <actions string="Single Action" />  The vescavor queen causes any number of vescavor swarms within 100 feet to immediately use their reaction to perform the Ravenous Bites action.

**Opportune Snack** <actions string="Single Action" />  The vescavor queen pulls a creature it has [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) into a space adjacent to it and makes a jaws Strike with a +2 circumstance bonus.

**Rage Pheromones**   If the vescavor queen's spit Strike damages a creature, it takes a –2 status penalty to all saving throws imposed by vescavor swarms for 1 minute.

</column>

markdown:
In their true form, a vilderavn is a great raven with a wingspan of 6–8 feet. Adaptable shapeshifters, they can change to the fighting forms of a snarling wolf, a hybrid of both wolf and raven, and a tall humanoid in black armor with a massive greatsword. More sinister is their ability to assume a humanoid guise suited to insinuating themselves into the retinues of boastful mortal rulers. With historical knowledge and clever rumormongering, they goad the proud into squabbles, feuds, and ultimately wars. The vilderavn stays at the ruler's side until victory is within grasp, the war almost won, then exacts the cruel stroke of betrayal. Their magic turns the mortals against each other, and the vilderavn's sword falls swiftly. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Vilderavn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3229) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 340 **Perception** +28; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +30, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +32, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +29, [Heraldry Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +26, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +24, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +32, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +26 **Str** +8 **Dex** +6 **Con** +5 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +30 **Will** +28 **HP** 300 **Immunities** [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 10 **Aura of Disquietude** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 30 feet, DC 35. As [frightful presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64), plus a creature [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) by the aura becomes suspicious; it doesn't count any other creature as its ally and can't [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292) or flank. On a critical failure, the creature also can't be a willing target for harmless or helpful magic.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** _greatsword_ +34 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 3d12+16 slashing plus bloodbird **Melee** jaws +32 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d8+16 piercing plus bloodbird **Melee** talon +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d8+16 slashing plus bloodbird **Occult Innate Spells** DC 37 - **5th** [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1620) (at will), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) (at will), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724), [Wave of Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757) (at will), [Rewrite Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655) - **8th** [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) - **Constant (6th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Bloodbird** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) A creature hit by a vilderavn's melee attack becomes cursed. It takes 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) that's difficult to stanch. The DC to stop the bleeding using [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2396) is 35, and healing the creature to full HP doesn't automatically end the bleeding. Removing the curse ends the bleeding. **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The vilderavn takes on the appearance of a Small or Medium humanoid, [wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=415), [dire wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=416), or hybrid with both raven and wolf parts. The vilderavn can only use their jaws attack when in a form with a wolf's head, and their talon attack in a form with raven qualities. They can instead assume their raven knight form: a Medium humanoid in black full plate carrying a greatsword. They can use their jaws or talon Strikes only in a form that has that body part, and their greatsword only in knight form. **Souleater** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) If the vilderavn kills a [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) with a critical hit using their jaws Strike, they rip out and devour the target's heart and soul as part of the attack. While the target is dead, the vilderavn can Change Shape into the target's form, gaining a +4 status bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) the target. If magic would resurrect the creature, the caster must succeed at a DC 34 [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check to extract the target's soul from the vilderavn; otherwise, the spell fails.
The Creation of Vilderavns Legends say a fey lord created the vilderavns as a weapon against those who ruined the land with their iron armaments. Vilderavns would exploit the hubris of mortals and devour their souls after death. With this goal, vilderavns hid in various forms to watch and learn human ways, and they executed their calling with great subtlety but no mercy. No mortal knows if this legend is true or spread by vilderavns to imply a purpose for their heartless cruelty.
name: Vilderavn

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Wargs hunt in packs like [wolves](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=485), but their penchant for taunting victims makes them easy to distinguish from their cousins. [Orcs](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=454) and [hobgoblins](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=430) frequently recruit wargs. Most wargs are amenable to this arrangement, but if food should run out, wargs are more than willing to integrate goblinoid flesh into their diet.

markdown:
A warsworn is an animate mass of corpses composed of dozens, sometimes even hundreds, of victims of battle. They are formed by deities of undeath or war or, rarely, spontaneously manifest from the devastation of an especially horrendous battle. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Warsworn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3232) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 342 **Perception** +27; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +7 **Int** -1 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +25 **Will** +27 +1 status to all saves vs. [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) **HP** 350 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Animated Weapons** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) 100 feet. The warsworn automatically controls unattended weapons in the aura, which levitate around the warsworn. The warsworn can telekinetically wield these weapons to make melee Strikes with a reach of 100 feet. These strikes deal four of the weapon's damage dice +9 and use the weapon's damage type.
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 100 feet, DC 35
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** corpse wave +32 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 4d12+9 bludgeoning plus energy drain **Melee** animated weapon +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** see animated weapons **Ranged** scrap ball +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d12+9 bludgeoning plus plummet **Absorb** ([Death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Trigger** The warsworn moves into a [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) creature's space; **Effect** The warsworn absorbs the dying creature into itself, instantly killing the creature and healing the warsworn for a number of Hit Points equal to the creature's level. As long as the warsworn still exists, absorbed creatures can't be resurrected except by [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) or a similarly powerful effect. **Energy Drain** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) When a warsworn hits with a corpse wave Strike or damages a creature with Trample, the target must succeed at a DC 35 Fortitude save or become [drained 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) and [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67). On a critical success, the target becomes temporarily immune to the warsworn's energy drain for 24 hours. **Plummet** A creature hit by a warsworn's scrap ball Strike must attempt a DC 37 Reflex save. On a failure, the target falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88); if the target was airborne, it [falls](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) up to 120 feet, taking damage from the fall and landing prone if the descent brings it to the ground. On a critical failure, the target is also held under a pile of scrap ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC 37). **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)** Huge or smaller, corpse wave, DC 37
Alternate Warsworns While uniquely terrible, war is not the only tragedy that can lead to mass deaths. Other forms of mass undead, similar to warsworn, can sometimes arise from causes such as famine or disaster. Flamesworn rise from large crowds killed by fire, while plagueborn rise when entire townships or even cities perish to disease.
name: Warsworn

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Wasp Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3234)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Wasp nests are made of chewed wood fibers foraged from the surrounding flora, which the wasps make into a material similar to paper. A single wasp nest can house thousands of individuals that emerge as a massive swarm. Most swarms attack only to protect their nest or if otherwise agitated, though druids and other primal enchanters can bend these venomous vermin to their will—to deadly effect.

markdown:
Wererats tend to be selfishly opportunistic, avaricious, and paranoid as a result of their curse. Because wererats typically dwell in metropolitan areas where they can hide in plain sight, practically any city goer could be a wererat in disguise—from the quiet shopkeep to the city's criminal mastermind. The bustle of crowds and countless rat holes make ghettos and shantytowns favored homes for wererats, especially since in these poorer districts the wererat can kill out of greed or fear with little chance of the authorities noticing. In some cities, wererats operate entire thieves' guilds or organized crime rings, and membership requires willfully submitting to the wererat's cursed bite. Wererats look very similar to [ratfolk](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=463) when in hybrid form, apart from potential differences in size, but ratfolk have no love for wererats. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 16 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Wererat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3235) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 345 **Perception** +8; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); rat empathy **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +8, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +6, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +5, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +4, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +8 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Rat Empathy** The wererat can communicate with rodents. **Items** [Hand Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=69) (20 bolts), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43) --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +10 **Will** +8 **HP** 45 **Weaknesses** [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5 **Nimble Dodge** **Trigger** A creature targets the wererat with an attack and the wererat can see the attacker; **Effect** The wererat gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.
 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** shortsword +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6+4 piercing **Melee** claw +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 1d6+2 slashing **Melee** jaws +10 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 1d8+2 piercing plus curse of the wererat **Ranged** hand crossbow +10 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6 piercing **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) Human with fist +10 for 1d4+2 bludgeoning, or Small rat with Speed 30 feet, climb 10 feet **Curse of the Wererat** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Saving Throw** DC 15 Fortitude **Moon Frenzy** ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The wererat deals 1d6 extra precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Werewolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3236)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The curse of the werewolf—known as lycanthropy to many—instills in its carriers the hungry bloodlust and predatory instincts of the wolf. Werewolves tend to dwell on the fringes of society or in small settlements where, in their humanoid forms, they work as laborers, hunters, farmers, or trappers. At night, however, these same villagers transform into violent killers and sadistic stalkers who prey on their neighbors. Werewolves are the quintessential werecreature, and the first that comes to mind when most people speak of such beings.

 Although most werewolves hide their curse by adopting solitary lifestyles, some retain the pack mentality of true wolves. A small group of such werewolves typically forms a family-like pack, with the eldest or most powerful werewolf serving as the leader. New pack mates are hand-chosen and inculcated into the family as its influence grows.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Werebear](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3237)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Unwavering conviction fills a werebear during their transformations. This can drive them zealously into noble causes, but it can also make them ruthless, violent, and single-minded. Alliances can fall from a werebear's mind as their bestial temper overcomes them and their goal overwhelms all. As a result, werebears are loners, rarely even living together as families longer than necessary. As long as there is nothing around to threaten it or the natural area it protects (typically a forest), a werebear in its animal form is generally content to forage and sleep away the night.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Weretiger](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3238)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These ferocious werecreatures stalk prey with the cunning and skill of a true apex predator. Weretigers typically view all life as a potential meal. Powerful nocturnal hunters with excellent senses that help them ambush prey, weretigers are adaptable to an extreme range of environments. However, weretigers living in densely populated cities (potentially as courtesans, assassins, or guild leaders) often struggle to suppress their killer instincts, becoming overwhelmed by the urge to hunt.

markdown:
Wights are intelligent undead spawned through inescapable cycles of spite. This spite might come from their own malevolent will in life, or can be instilled by necromantic rituals, typically involving the desecration of burial sites. Wights usually haunt burial grounds, catacombs, or other places of the dead. Their hunger is targeted toward the living—those whose pumping hearts and ruddy warmth inspire visceral hatred. As many types of wights exist as types of people from which they might be created. Hulking brutes, skittering sneaks, and cunning tinkers all make for different wights with different niches to fill. The environment, too, plays a part in determining a wight's special abilities and defenses. Frost wights, for instance, can be found in parts of the world where exposure is a common end and the resentment of being left alone in the wild is a common source of spite. Durable and sustained by void energy, wights can last in harsh environments without decaying the way some lesser undead do. A single wight can wreak significant havoc if it is compelled to rise from its tomb. Because creatures slain under a wight's curse can become wights as well, all it takes is a single wight and a handful of unlucky graveyard visitors to create a veritable horde of these undead. Thus, canny priests and adventurers know that the best solution to a wight problem is swift and total eradication. Care must be taken, though, to destroy wight spawn before attempting to destroy the parent wight, for spawn without a master gain the ability to create spawn of their own. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Wight](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3239) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 348 **Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +8 **Str** +4 **Dex** +1 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Grave Weapon** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The wight is bound to a dagger it was buried with. Other wights can be bound to different weapons. **Items** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +6 **Will** +10 **HP** 40 (fueled by spite, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Final Spite** **Trigger** The wight is reduced to 0 Hit Points; **Effect** The wight makes a Strike before being destroyed. This Strike can inflict corrupting spite, but fueled by spite doesn't apply.
**Fueled by Spite** Each time a creature loses Hit Points due to a corrupting spite curse the wight inflicted, the wight gains 3 temporary Hit Points. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** dagger +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+6 piercing plus corrupting spite **Melee** claw +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 1d4+6 slashing plus corrupting spite **Ranged** dagger +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+6 slashing plus corrupting spite **Corrupting Spite** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The wight's [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) attacks and bound weapons inflict a curse that makes a creature grow weak and spiteful. If a wight inflicts corrupting spite on a creature already afflicted by it, the victim attempts a new save, ignoring the result if it's better than a failure. A living humanoid that dies while under the curse rises as a wight after 1d4 rounds, [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) by the wight that killed it. The wight spawn can't inflict corrupting spite and is [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61). If its creator dies or after roughly a month of existence, the new wight becomes autonomous and turns into a normal wight; **Saving Throw** DC 17 Fortitude; **Stage 1** [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) (1 round); **Stage 2** drained 2 and doesn't treat any creatures as allies (1 round); **Stage 3** As stage 2, except drained 3 (1 round); **Stage 4** As stage 2, except drained 4 (1 round).

markdown:
Malevolent balls of colored light, will-o'-wisps haunt lonely marshes and forests, where they lure unsuspecting travelers into danger. Will-o'-wisps can vary the color and illumination they shed, and delight in mimicking bobbing lanterns or distant fires to draw lost or disoriented travelers off of safe trails. They can extinguish their illumination entirely to become invisible, and they enjoy doing so once their victims are wholly lost and have realized that the bobbing light in the distance isn't, in fact, leading them to safety. Even invisible, however, a will-o'-wisp rarely ventures far from its target, as it feasts upon the panic and dread felt by its victims. Beneath its glow, a will-o'-wisp's body is a spongy ball approximately 1 foot in diameter and weighing less than 5 pounds. Although most will-o'-wisps are merely translucent, featureless orbs, gaining definition only in the shifting illumination they create, a few have dark mottling that makes them resemble a skull when viewed closely. Will-o'-wisps have no need for mundane nourishment, and in fact, lack the ability to consume matter of any kind; they find all the sustenance they need in the terror of nearby creatures. For this reason, they like to work alongside undead that produce terror in their victims. Will-o'-wisps are long-lived, if not effectively immortal, and they have good memories. A cowed or defeated will-o'-wisp can be a good source of lore and information, though acquiring such cooperation from such a sinister monster is no easy feat. Will-o'-wisps inhabit desolate swamps and forests and are generally active at twilight and after dark. They are therefore reluctant to lead victims into immediately fatal areas such as deadfalls, but instead prefer hazards where their victims suffer over a long time, such as pockets of stale or poisonous air, patches of quicksand, and dens of bigger monsters. According to will-o'-wisps, different types of fear have subtle differences in flavor. The lurking dread in the pit of the stomach that gnaws at those who slowly become aware of the fact that they're lost produces a much different taste than the sudden, stark terror of imminent death in the face of a towering monster. Because of this, will-o'- wisps try to vary the ways in which they induce terror in their prey, to ensure they don't tire of certain flavors of fear. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Will-O'-Wisp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3240) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 349 **Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +18, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +16 **Str** -5 **Dex** +6 **Con** +0 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +16 **Will** +14 **HP** 50 **Immunities** magic **Glow** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640)) 20 feet. A will-o'-wisp is itself naturally [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), but glows with a colored light, casting [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in the aura and making it visible.
**Magic Immunity** A will-o'-wisp is immune to all spells except [_force barrage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [_quandary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644), and [_revealing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653). --- **Speed** fly 50 feet **Melee** shock +17 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+4 electricity **Feed on Fear** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** An enemy within 15 feet is under a [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effect or [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69); **Effect** The will-o'-wisp feeds on the creature's terror. It regains 2d4 Hit Points, and if it has Gone Dark, its glow reignites. **Go Dark** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The will-o'-wisp extinguishes its glow, becoming [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83). It can end this effect with another use of this action. If it uses its shock attack while invisible, the arc of electricity lets any observer determine its location, making the will-o'-wisp only [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) to all observers until it moves.
Eyes of the Dead The elder goddess [Nhimbaloth](/Deities.aspx?ID=163), the so-called “Empty Death,” is said by many to be the source of all will-o'-wisps. Cultists of Nhimbaloth claim she has no true form but is merely a presence that can be felt by all in danger of a pointless and futile death. These same cultists maintain that all will-o'-wisps are Nhimbaloth's eyes, and it's through these fear-devouring creatures that she looks upon all worlds from an eldritch realm beyond even the very concept of death.
name: Will-O'-Wisp

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3241)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Wolves live and hunt in packs, which, contrary to popular belief, are not led by the strongest in the group, but typically consist of a mated pair, their pups, and juvenile offspring from previous mating seasons. Offspring generally leave their parents' pack upon reaching maturity, at which point they seek out mates of their own to form their own packs elsewhere.

 Humanoids are not traditionally viewed as prey animals by wolves, but extraordinary circumstances can lead these animals to attack people, especially in winter months and other situations where traditional prey (deer and elk mainly) is scarce. Some beings, such as powerful [vampires](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=480), can call upon wolves to aid them in combat.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Dire Wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3242)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Much larger and more foul-tempered than their common cousins, dire wolves haunt primeval lands that accommodate their massive size and proportionately large hunting grounds and appetites. Orcs are fond of using dire wolves as mounts, finding their vicious tempers perfect for hunting and warfare. Dire wolves are far more likely to prey on humanoids than ordinary wolves, considering them nothing but another kind of smaller, nutritious animal.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Wraith](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3243)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Wraiths are malevolent undead who drain life and shun light. Their shadowy forms are covered by insubstantial robes that they wear like a badge of office and marked with peering eyes that reflect their judgment of the living. A wraith can be created by foul magic or direct exposure to [the Void](/Planes.aspx?ID=28), but more often they are the result of death on a tragic scale. When a tragedy is too great for even reality to witness, a temporary manifestation of the Void can leave behind countless wraiths in a horde of darkness. A wraith's existence is one of emptiness and need, with a desire to call others to the same emptiness exemplified by the Void.

 Wraiths can haunt any location where they can safely interact with the living, looking for those worthy to become new wraiths and disposing of the rest, though their vulnerability to sunlight confines them to the shadowy places of the world—places where they can blend in seamlessly with their dark surroundings before silently engulfing their prey. Wraiths' opinions on who is worthy vary, but they typically choose those closest to the Void already, whether from a metaphysical connection or exposure to countless deaths. A new wraith created this way carries these aspects, with the rest of their personality warped or scoured away by exposure to the Void.

 Wraiths gather with others of their kind in places where death and mayhem are commonplace—countrysides ravaged by war, metropolitan underworlds run by criminal overlords, or sites of fiendish rituals. In these places, the living do well to keep to the light. Wraiths are smart enough to take advantage of their incorporeality in combat, so they keep to tortuous caverns or structures with hallways, and avoid open areas.

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

</column>

The target takes 6d6 mental damage with a DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save. On a critical failure, the creature is also [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) until the start of the wraith's next turn.

**Robes of Welcome** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The wraith wraps its robes around an adjacent living creature, exposing it to void's embrace. markdown:
Nearly a myth, a yeti is rarely seen—and even when it is, it is often too late. Yetis dwell amid the highest, most remote peaks of the world, coming down from their snowy mountain holds to raid, steal livestock, and sometimes feed their insatiable urges for slaughter and destruction. Those folks who live at the foot of a yeti-ruled mountain warn of the “abominable snowmen”: monstrous, fur-covered humanoids who leave strange and bloody tracks in the snow. In actuality, most yetis protect the world rather than hunt its other denizens, guarding eldritch portals that link the mortal Universe and other, much stranger dimensions of reality. From within these snow-covered arches and ancient stone doorways, aliens, living nightmares, fiends, and worse can emerge into the world. Yetis who guard these portals sometimes succumb to the horrors within, taking on the bloodthirsty urges and horrific behaviors of the very monsters they strive to guard against. Driven out of their clans and forced to wander alone, they give rise to the myth of the abominable snowman. These exiled yetis often fully embrace the corrupting elements that caused their exile in the first place, growing more powerful and more deadly. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Yeti](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3247) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 354 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +11 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** -1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** -1 **Snowblind** When [Hiding](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404), the yeti is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) by any snowfall, even if it's not thick enough to make other creatures concealed. --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +11 **Will** +13 +4 status to all saves vs. [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) and [dreams](/Traits.aspx?ID=582) **HP** 115 **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10 **Nightmare Guardian** Yetis gain a +4 status bonus to saves against fear and against spells and abilities that affect dreams. A yeti who falls prey to a supernatural nightmare loses this ability and becomes permanently enraged, gaining a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls and a –1 status penalty to AC.
**Vanish** **Trigger** The yeti is [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) while not in combat, and a creature would observe it; **Effect** The yeti Strides or [Climbs](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) up to half its Speed to a location where it can Hide, then Hides. If its new [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) check result meets or exceeds the triggering creature's Perception DC, the yeti remains hidden. --- **Speed** 35 feet, climb 20 feet; arctic passage **Melee** claw +15, **Damage** 2d10+5 slashing **Arctic Passage** The yeti ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) caused by ice or snow. **Grisly Arrival** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** The yeti hits a creature in the first round of combat and the yeti was [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) from that creature at the start of combat; **Effect** Each enemy within 30 feet that witnesses the attack (including the target of the attack) must attempt a DC 23 Will save. On a failure, the creature is [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76); on a critical failure, it's frightened 4. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=73)** claw
Yeti Crafts Lone exiles of yeti clans have little use for treasure and leave the gear of their slain victims behind, where it is quickly covered by snowfall. Clan-based yetis, on the other hand, create beautifully carved stonework, some of which they shape into protective talismans of rare beauty
name: Yeti

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Zecui](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3248)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These chitinous entities can only grow to adulthood within a host creature, usually a humanoid or larger animal. When such hosts are particularly plentiful, zecuis can multiply at a horrifying rate, sealing hosts in subterranean chambers until their larvae grow to adulthood. However, during lean times, zecui adults will sate their hunger by eating those hosts along with any unlucky larvae gestating within, unable to be infested by their own young.

 Between periods of wakefulness, zecuis hibernate for decades in burrows or buried within the soil. Sometimes an unincubated larva will be buried this way, waiting for a living host to come in contact with it. Once a zecui larva has gestated long enough to take control of its host, it may seek out larger and more powerful entities to devour the host, transferring themselves to a more plentiful source of food.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Zecui.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=523" />
</traits>

**Skills**

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +5

**Con** +2

**Int** +0

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
[Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43) (2)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 23 

**Fort** +14 

**Ref** +17 

**Will** +12 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 110

</row>

**Preserve Prey** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Trigger** A living creature within 30 feet is reduced to 0 Hit Points; **Effect** The zecui channels corrupt vitality into the triggering creature, which still goes [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) but does not gain the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition. While that creature is unconscious, the residual energy attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) spell healing that creature with a +15 counteract modifier.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, burrow 20 feet, climb 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
mandibles +15,
**Damage** 2d8+7 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortsword +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 2d6+7 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claws +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 2d4+7 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
spit +17 ([range 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** spit mucus

**Dual Stab** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The zecui makes two shortsword Strikes against an [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) or [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) target. These strikes count towards the zecui's multiple attack penalty, but it doesn't increase until after the second attack.

**Harden Chitin** <actions string="Single Action" />  The zecui fuses much of their chitin into a black metallic shell. They gain resistance 5 to all damage (except [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) and [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) until they next take a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action.

**Spit Mucus**   A creature hit by the zecui's spit attack is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) by the larva-infested mucus and stuck to the nearest surface until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 25). While that creature is immobilized, it is exposed to zecui larvae at the end of each of its turns.

**Zecui Larvae**  ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) **Saving Throw** DC 25 Fortitude; **Stage 1** visible lumps as the larvae move but no ill effect (1 day); **Stage 2** [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) (1 day); **Stage 3** drained 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** drained 3 and [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) by the zecui larva (1 day); **Stage 5** the creature dies and the adult zecui can emerge from the corpse as an Interact action

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">The Dark Brood</title>

Although zecuis generally operate in personal nests, a coalition of zecui thrives in the Valashmai Jungle of Tian Xia, nesting in the ruins of the ancient empire that attempted to control their ancestors. Recently, the brood has turned its gaze outwards, building up numbers to begin a great expansion.
</aside>
name: Zecui

markdown:
An almiraj closely resembles a yellow hare in almost all ways... except for the 2-foot-long black horn jutting from its forehead. Though it shares this trait with the docile [unicorn](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=504), that's where their similarities end. Almirajes are vicious creatures, known to slaughter much larger cattle and, in times of desperation, people. Its stare is said to send even the bravest warrior running for their life. Unlike common hares, almirajes are solitary and intensely territorial. They're adaptable ambush hunters with the stamina and speed to chase down any prey. They attack with their jaws and claws but are primarily known for trying to gut their prey with a vicious stab from their horn. Many scholars believe that the terrifying magical aura the creatures exude originates from their horns. This line of thinking has led to an increase in almiraj attacks as local citizens try to capture the creature alive and remove the horn, hoping this will turn it into a more docile hare. These hopes have not proven fruitful, as an almiraj is just as aggressive, if not more so, after their horn has been removed. Adventurers often think themselves lucky if they manage to avoid the terrifying horn of this ferocious predator, but it's a much better alternative than being caught in its jaws. Almirajes have a nasty habit of grabbing their prey and vigorously shaking them until they go limp. If this fails, they'll drag their prey underground until they suffocate deep in the earth. Few have ever escaped the burrow of an almiraj. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Almiraj](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3253) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 124 **Perception** +14; [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 40 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +8 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** -4 **Wis** +0 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +11 **Will** +8 **HP** 63 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 5 feet, DC 22. Creatures that critically fail are also [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) for 1 round.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** Horn only. [Fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) creatures take an additional 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). On a critical hit, the target is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). --- **Speed** 35 feet, burrow 25 feet **Melee** horn +13, **Damage** 2d8+7 slashing **Melee** jaws +13, **Damage** 2d6+7 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** claw +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d4+7 slashing **Final Shake** **Requirements** The almiraj has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77); **Effect** The almiraj clamps its jaws down on the creature and shakes it vigorously, attempting to finish it off. The creature takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage and must succeed at a DC 20 Fortitude save or become [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71). Small or smaller creatures take a –2 circumstance penalty to their save. **Goring Charge** The almiraj lowers its head and moves with ferocity toward its selected prey. The almiraj Strides twice. If it ends its movement within melee reach of at least one enemy, it makes a horn Strike against that enemy. This Strike deals an additional 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). **Into the Earth** **Requirements** The almiraj has a Medium or smaller creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77); **Effect** The almiraj attempts to flee with its meal, burrowing into the ground. The grabbed creature must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or be dragged beneath the ground as the almiraj [Burrows](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) up to its Speed. If the target succeeds at its save, it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) and the almiraj continues burrowing as normal; if it fails, the grabbed creature must hold its breath or begin [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=2436) under the dirt.
name: Almiraj

These giant bees feed on the nectar of medicinal herbs and magical flowers, naturally processing the liquid into syrupy-sweet potions. Depending on the precise diet of the apothecary bees, they can produce a wide variety of potions, storing any excess in the waxy cells lining their nest. Apothecary bee workers are about a foot long, with yellow and black stripes. While carrying a potion, the bee's abdomen visibly swells and often changes color to match the potion within. The value of the apothecary bees' potions has led to numerous attempts at domestication, a dangerous, expensive dream with both high risks and high rewards. Once a magical garden has been established, apothecary beehives tend to become self-sustaining. Beekeepers can potentially guide their cultivation, but they're no longer necessary for the hive's survival. Some ancient, long-abandoned gardens still grow along the Sphinx River in Osirion, where many apothecary bees prowl for flowers that meet their standards. Upon their most recent dig to the surface, [surki](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=76) communities quickly began the process of “trading” with the bees, establishing habitats and using their natural magic sense to better tend the massive fields a large hive needs. [Miflits](/Monsters.aspx?ID=245), on the other hand, often end up absorbed into the bees' social structure. The potions a hive produces vary from season to season, depending on what flowers are available. Beekeepers have made strides in creating gardens that yield specific potions and have even developed harvesting techniques that increase potion effectiveness. These techniques are valuable, closely guarded secrets, but even defective potions with cursed results have found use among the followers of [Calistria](/Deities.aspx?ID=279) and [Norgorber](/Deities.aspx?ID=289). **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Apothecary Bee](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3254) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 125 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), potionsight ([precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411)) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +15 **Str** +1 **Dex** +6 **Con** +3 **Int** -4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 **Internal Cauldron** With an appropriate diet, an apothecary bee will create a potion of 6th level or lower every two weeks. Most often, this is a [_moderate healing potion_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2943). Each apothecary bee can store only one completed potion at a time. Unused potions can generally be harvested from an apothecary bee with a successful DC 23 [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) or [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) check.
**Potionsight** Apothecary bees' complex eyes let them see the auras of magical [potions](/Traits.aspx?ID=672) and instinctively identify potions of their level or lower. These auras are visible through mundane containers, though their eyes have no special ability to perceive or identify non-magical solutions, such as alchemical [elixirs](/Traits.aspx?ID=589). **Items** [_moderate healing potion_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2943) --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +19 **Will** +13 **HP** 115 **Weaknesses** smoke susceptibility **Smoke Susceptibility** An apothecary bee is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) if it starts its turn in heavy smoke. --- **Speed** 10 feet, climb 10 feet, fly 50 feet **Melee** stinger +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)), **Damage** 1d4+4 piercing plus 3d6 poison **Drain Potion** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The apothecary bee has no stored potions; **Effect** The apothecary bee slurps up a potion within its reach, storing it internally. A creature can attempt a DC 25 Reflex save to protect a potion in its possession. **Ingest Potion** The apothecary bee consumes the potion it has stored in its body. If the potion has the [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) trait, the apothecary bee heals for the maximum amount. **Inject Potion** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The apothecary bee injects its stored potion into a willing creature within its reach. This deals 1 piercing damage and grants the recipient the normal effects of drinking the potion.
name: Apothecary Bee

The royal basilisk is aptly named, not for only the metal crown upon its head but also for the unique potency of its poison. The creature's poison manifests as a sort of cloying vapor that sticks onto and eats away at anything it touches. Their mastery of poison does appear to come at a cost, as the royal basilisk has no ability to petrify its prey. Although the royal basilisk can painstakingly shape a crown, some made of simple iron and others of rare skymetals, they much prefer to collect crowns crafted by other creatures. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Royal Basilisk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3255) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 127 **Perception** +27; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 120 feet, [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) ([precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411)) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +24, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +24, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +25 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +7 **Int** -3 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +2 **Items** iron crown --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +22 **Will** +23 **HP** 290 **Immunities** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) **Resistances** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) 15 **Crowned Royalty** The royal basilisk's crown is firmly attached to its head but can be [Disarmed](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) as though it were a held item. Without a crown, the royal basilisk's mastery over poison is weakened enough that it loses its miasmatic shroud. The royal basilisk can equip a crown within its tail's reach as an Interact action. A royal basilisk's crown is normally made of iron and enables the basilisk to use miasmatic shroud, but crowns made of other, more exotic materials might confer different abilities.
**Miasmatic Shroud** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) 15 feet. The poison in the breath of the royal basilisk makes the air around it a haze, [concealing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) it from all creatures outside the aura, but it cannot use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405). When a creature ends its turn within the aura, it is exposed to royal basilisk venom. --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** jaws +27 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+11 piercing plus Improved Grab and royal basilisk venom **Melee** tail +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+11 bludgeoning **Ranged** spit +24 ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [range 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 5d10 poison plus royal basilisk venom **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=65)** 4d8+3 bludgeoning, DC 32 **Royal Basilisk Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 36; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d10 poison and [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d10 poison and clumsy 3 (1 round); **Stage 3** 4d10 poison, clumsy 3, and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) (1 round) **Stone-Hewing Spit** ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524)) The royal basilisk spits its poison with immense force, dealing 5d10 acid and 5d8 piercing damage (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save) to creatures in a 240-foot line and exposing each creature that took damage to royal basilisk venom. The line penetrates barriers with Hardness of less than 20, ignoring any bonuses they'd provide from cover. The royal basilisk can't use Stone-Hewing Spit again for 1d4 rounds. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** Large, 5d10 acid damage, Rupture 30 **Wrap in Coils** **Requirements** A Large or smaller creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) in the royal basilisk's jaws; **Effect** The royal basilisk moves the creature into its coils, freeing its jaws to make attacks, then uses [Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=65) against the creature. The royal basilisk's coils can hold as many creatures as will fit in its space.
name: Royal Basilisk

The pestilent blossoms adorning the antlers of a blooming guardian are most beautiful in moonlight, when they achieve full bloom and reveal their celestial glow. When blooming guardians near the end of their life cycle, they walk one final patrol of their forest as their ancient body decomposes into the understory. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Blooming Guardian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3256) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 128 **Perception** +29; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411)) 40 feet **Languages** [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1680), [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +21, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +29, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +27 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 **Petal Form** The blooming guardian can dissipate momentarily into a swirling cloud of petals and pollen to pass over brambles and slip through cracks. When Striding in woodland terrain, the blooming guardian ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and can pass through obstacles so long as there is a small passageway. --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +23 **Will** +25 **HP** 360 **Resistances** [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 15 **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 15 **[Buck](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=54)** DC 36 --- **Speed** 50 feet **Melee** antlers +28, **Damage** 4d12+5 piercing **Melee** hooves +26, **Damage** 4d10+5 bludgeoning plus [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 33 - **3rd** [Animal Vision](/Spells.aspx?ID=1963) - **Constant (4th)** [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=1680), [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681) **Budding Siphon** ([Aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Stance](/Traits.aspx?ID=701), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) 20 feet. A blooming guardian is in a constant state of decomposition; to keep this entropy at bay, the flowers that cover their antlers continually siphon life from the surrounding area. All living creatures that enter or start their turn in the emanation take 6d4 void damage (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save).
At the start of their turn in any round that the blooming guardian deals damage with siphoning buds, they gain 10 temporary Hit Points, plus 2 temporary Hit Points for each creature damaged beyond the first since the blooming guardian's last turn, to a maximum of 20 temporary Hit Points. **Full Bloom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Stance](/Traits.aspx?ID=701)) The flowers on the blooming guardian's antlers bloom, unveiling their brilliance as they eat away at their host. While in this stance, the blooming guardian's Strikes deal an additional 2d4 poison damage plus 5 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29); on a hit, a target must succeed at a DC 36 Fortitude save or become [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71). The blooming guardian takes 5 persistent poison damage as long as it's in Full Bloom. **Goring Charge** The blooming guardian Strides twice and makes an antlers Strike after either Stride. If the Strike hits, the blooming guardian deals an extra 2d12 bludgeoning damage, and the target takes a –2 circumstance penalty to its next Fortitude save against blossom siphon. **Wail of the Forest** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Requirements** The blooming guardian is in Full Bloom; **Effect** The blooming guardian confronts their mortality, causing their flowers to burst and spray their pollen. All creatures in a 40-foot cone take 10d10 void damage with a DC 36 Fortitude save. The blooming guardian then exits Full Bloom and can't enter it again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** No effect.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68). Creatures that are [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) lose that condition and increase the drained condition by the same value.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but double damage.
name: Blooming Guardian

A greater chimera can have any combination of three heads chosen from the options below, each granting additional abilities inherent in the original creature. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Greater Chimera](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3257) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 133 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +23 **Will** +20 **HP** 235 **Multiple Reactions** A greater chimera gains 2 extra reactions each round that it can use only to make Reactive Strikes. It must use a different head for each reaction, and it can't use more than one on the same triggering action. If it loses one of its heads, it also loses one of these extra reactions.
**Three-Headed** Any ability that would sever a greater chimera's head (such as a critical hit with a [_vorpal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2853) weapon) severs one head at random. Losing a head doesn't kill a chimera (as long as it has at least one head left), but it does prevent it from making Strikes with the lost head or using any abilities granted by the head.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** claw +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 3d8+8 slashing **Three-Headed Strike** The greater chimera makes a Strike with each of its heads, each at a –2 penalty and targeting a different creature. These Strikes count as only one attack for the greater chimera's multiple attack penalty, and the penalty doesn't increase until after it has made all three attacks.
name: Greater Chimera

These small insects are a nuisance and danger to small villages within Brevoy, taking over entire forests and blending in with the foliage. Also known as calling beetles, due to their ability to mimic and repeat the voices of their previous victims, crying cicadas wait for passersby to lure into the trees, fascinating their victims with their cries before fluttering their wings to release toxic dust. Once a target is near death, crying cicadas will take flight from the trees and land on the body to feed. The scale dust of crying cicadas can be turned into an effective ingested poison, but obtaining a cicada and harvesting these scales is so dangerous that most would-be poisoners simply die in the process. Even finding the insects is dangerous enough; crying cicadas' natural stealth and dark coloration make them difficult to locate among the trees of a forest, and following their cries often leads one to fall victim to the very creatures they're hunting. The longer a colony of crying cicadas stays in a forest, the wider their collection of cries grows. Often, a young colony will be able to replicate only the sounds of small, dying creatures, but larger, more established colonies will have the ability to mimic the sounds of children and dragons. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Crying Cicada](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3258) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 134 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +8 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** -5 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +3 **Wings Flat** When the crying cicada is still and perched on a tree, it blends seamlessly into the environment. It has an automatic result of 30 on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks and DCs to pass as part of the tree. --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +12 **Will** +7 **HP** 48 **Immunities** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) --- **Speed** 15 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** proboscis +9, **Damage** 1d8+5 piercing plus 1d4 poison **Melee** slam +9, **Damage** 1d6+4 bludgeoning plus crying cicada poison **Crying Cicada Poison** ([Inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=634), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 19 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage plus [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d6 poison damage plus [slowed 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) (1 round); **Stage 3** 2d6 poison damage plus slowed 2 and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) (1 round). **Sob** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The crying cicada mimics the noise of a wounded animal or crying child. Non-cicada creatures within a 150-foot emanation must attempt a DC 19 Will save or be distressed by the pleas for help. The effect lasts for 1 round, but if the cicada uses this ability again on subsequent rounds, it extends the duration by 1 round for all affected creatures. Once a creature succeeds at any save against Sob, that creature is temporarily immune to Sob for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature believes an animal or child needs help somewhere nearby. The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72), and it must spend each of its actions to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) or move closer to the cicada as expediently as possible, while avoiding obvious dangers. If the creature is adjacent to the cicada, it stays still and doesn't act. **Steal Voice** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541)) **Requirements** An enemy creature has spoken since the crying cicada's last turn; **Effect** The crying cicada learns and mimics the sound of its opponent's voice. It can't make new sentences, but it can choose to repeat select parts of the phrases it has heard. All non-cicada creatures within 30 feet, other than the owner of the stolen voice, must succeed at a DC 19 Will save to disbelieve the mimicry. **Wing Flurry** The crying cicada beats its wings together, exposing all creatures within a 10-foot burst to crying cicada poison.
name: Crying Cicada

<title level="1" pfs="">[Decapod Dinghy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3259)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Decapod dinghies are massive crabs indigenous to the Okaiyo Ocean that often assist the local humanoids with transportation. They can understand [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) and are instantly recognizable by the colorful anemones they carry on their backs and in their front claws. [Athamarus](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=71) and [merfolk](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=74) are common sights atop decapod dinghies, lounging comfortably amidst the anemones and sharing food with their hosts.

 This peaceful cooperation is indirectly caused by the legendary crustacean [Tehialai-Thief-Of-Ships](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1741). Since Tehialai's rampages are a constant threat in the Okaiyo, the ancestors of the decapod dinghies bonded with other marine life to survive. Decapod dinghies' relationship with anemones is particularly tight, using the small creatures as poisonous extensions of their own bodies and using their response to movements in the water to compensate for the decapod dinghy's otherwise poor vision. In a region wracked by Tehialai's storms, everyone needs to band together, after all.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Decapod_Dinghy.webp" />
</column>

</row>

**Languages**
[Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113); (can't speak any language)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +15

**Cha** +0
</row>

**All Aboard** A decapod dinghy can carry up to 30 Bulk of creatures or unattended items on their back, such as five Medium creatures. The anemones' gentle hold on the passengers is sufficient in most situations, but while in combat, those passengers are [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) if they want to take other actions while remaining on the decapod dinghy's back.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

</row>

[poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)

bludgeoning 5

**Protect Passenger** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** An enemy within 20 feet damages one of the decapod dinghy's passengers; **Effect** The decapod dinghy's anemones lash out for a tentacle Strike against the triggering creature.

</column>

---

**Speed** 30 feet, climb 25 feet, swim 25 feet

tentacle +19 ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 5d6 poison plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Anemone Transport** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) The decapod dinghy and all their willing passengers teleport to a patch of anemones within 10 miles large enough for the decapod dinghy to stand among them. Although the decapod dinghy doesn't need to see the location, they must have been there before. All creatures transported are temporarily immune to this ability for 24 hours.

**Inflate Anemones** <actions string="Single Action" />  The anemones on the decapod dinghy's back inflate, enveloping all passengers and giving each passenger a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until the beginning of the decapod dinghy's next turn.

**Stinging Anemones** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) The decapod dinghy's anemones sting any number of creatures they choose among passengers and those the decapod dinghy has [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90). The stings deal 5d6 poison damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). On a failed save, that creature is also [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) for 1 hour.

</column>
name: Decapod Dinghy

A creature that fails this save is [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) until it recovers to full Hit Points.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Carnotaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3263)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

This 2,000-pound predator lives up to its name by ramming its prey with its distinctive horns lowered, knocking them prone, and viciously tearing into fallen victims before they can regain their footing. Do not let its size fool you; this massive dinosaur is deceptively quick when charging a potential meal. Aggressive and highly territorial, a carnotaurus will not hesitate to hunt down and slay any creature unlucky enough to cross its path.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Therizinosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3264)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

At first glance, the therizinosaurus, with its rotund, feathered body and bird-like beak, strikes many observers as almost comical. This impression tends to quickly fade at the sight of the creature's claws, six wicked, scythe-like blades capable of disemboweling a foe with a single well-placed slash. Despite their intimidating appearance, these enormous claws are used primarily to separate tasty leaves and branches from the tops of the tall trees upon which the therizinosaurus feeds. Nonetheless, travelers through a therizinosaurus's territory are wisely advised to give the creature a wide berth, less they wind up on the wrong end of its notoriously foul temper.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**

</column>

**Swiping Claws** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The therizinosaurus makes two claw Strikes against different targets. Both attacks count toward its multiple attack penalty, but do not increase the penalty until it has made both attacks.

**Winnowing Claws**   Whenever the therizinosaurus successfully Strikes a Large or smaller creature with its claw, it [pulls](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) that creature 5 feet toward it (10 feet on a critical hit).

Titanosaurs are some of the largest land animals ever to walk the surface of Golarion, massive sauropods that can reach over 100 feet in length and weigh more than 80 tons. While typically content to focus on consuming vast quantities of vegetation and ignore the presence of smaller creatures, titanosaurs that feel threatened can unleash destruction rivaling natural disasters. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 35 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 [Titanosaur](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3265) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 138 **Perception** +27; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +34 **Str** +10 **Dex** +4 **Con** +9 **Int** -4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 38 **Fort** +33 **Ref** +28 **Will** +29 **HP** 370 **Majestic Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 90 feet, DC 38. Creatures of Huge size or smaller that enter the aura must attempt a Will save. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune to the titanosaur's Majestic Presence for 1 hour.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) by the titanosaur for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is fascinated for 2 rounds.
**Critical Failure** The creature is fascinated for 1 minute. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** tail +32 ([reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+16 bludgeoning plus [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=28) **Melee** foot +32 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+16 bludgeoning **Seismic Slam** **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** The titanosaur rears up on its hind legs before slamming its forelegs back on the ground with a thunderous crash, creating a localized tremor with the effects of an [_earthquake_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1507) spell centered on itself, though the effect is non-magical. Fissures do not form under the titanosaur. **Sweeping Tail** The titanosaur lashes its tail in a 30-foot cone. Creatures in the area take 8d10 bludgeoning damage (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The momentum of the titanosaur's swing then makes it [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the beginning of its next turn. **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)** Huge or smaller, foot, DC 38
name: Titanosaur

The unholy terror known as the thruneosaurus rex is not a natural beast. They were created by Chelaxian diabolists who sought to combine the ferocity of dinosaurs with the power of [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42), infusing a clutch of [tyrannosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2923) rex eggs from Mediogalti Island with [devil](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=389) ichor and naming the monstrous hatchlings after their patrons. Although few records and fewer survivors tell exactly how the experiment went awry, the experimental subjects escaped, and their descendants are now most often sighted in the Whisperwood in eastern Cheliax. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Thruneosaurus Rex](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3266) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 139 **Perception** +29; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +30, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +35, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +30 **Str** +10 **Dex** +3 **Con** +6 **Int** -2 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +31 **Ref** +25 **Will** +29 **HP** 290 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) **Resistances** physical 15 (except [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379)) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 15 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 38
**Instinctive Teleportation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Trigger** The thruneosaurus takes damage from a [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) effect; **Effect** The thruneosaurus teleports to an open space within 60 feet it can see, along with any creatures it has swallowed whole. --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** jaws +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+16 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** foot +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+16 bludgeoning **Hellfire Blast** The thruneosaurus channels sulfurous hellfire in one of three ways. The thruneosaurus chooses the effect, but it can't make the same choice in two consecutive rounds.
  • **Blistering Sun** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)); The thruneosaurus ignites an orb of hellfire between its horns that radiates heat in a 30-foot emanation, dealing 2d8 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
  • **Choking Fumes** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)); The thruneosaurus exhales a 60-foot cone of sulfurous smoke that deals 6d8 poison damage (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). Creatures that fail their saves are [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91).
  • **Fiery Beam** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)); A 120-foot line of hellish flame erupts from the thruneosaurus's eyes, dealing 6d10 fire damage (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).
**[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) Huge, 3d12+10 bludgeoning, Rupture 40 **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)** Huge or smaller, foot, DC 38
name: Thruneosaurus Rex

Morthaks, being low, squat, and flat, resemble a crocodile rebuilt to dig like a mole. At home beneath vast meadows and plains, the only sign of their passing are the earthen mounds they leave behind when they emerge to hunt. Those who encounter them in the wild speak of the sub-aural rumble they produce as they disintegrate the earth with sonic blasts, a sound that's felt rather than heard. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Morthak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3267) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 140 **Perception** +12; no vision, sensitive echolocation ([precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411)) 90 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +10, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +12 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** -3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Sensitive Echolocation** The morthak can use its hearing as a [precise sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) with the listed range. If the morthak takes sonic damage beyond its resistance, its senses are overloaded and all creatures are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) from it for 1 round. --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +10 **Will** +10 **HP** 70 **Immunities** [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) **Resistances** sonic 5 **Auditory Swipe** **Trigger** A creature within the morthak's reach uses an [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541)
**action** **Effect** The morthak makes a claw Strike against the triggering creature. --- **Speed** 20 feet, burrow 30 feet **Melee** jaws +13, **Damage** 2d8+5 piercing **Melee** claw +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 1d6+5 slashing plus 1d6 sonic **Ranged** screech +10 ([range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)), **Damage** 4d6 sonic **Shattering Scrape** ([Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) The morthak makes a claw Strike against a creature wearing stone or metal armor. If the Strike hits and the armor's Hardness is 12 or lower, that armor is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60).
name: Morthak
