Drthaks are aquatic predators with long trailing fins that are rarely seen, clinging to coasts and estuaries where the high silt content makes them practically invisible to predators and prey alike. Some have been known to come inland, tracking their prey miles upriver before striking. The underwater environment alters the typical dischoran hunting behavior, with drthaks creating bubbles that implode to stun prey. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Drthak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3268) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 140 **Perception** +15; no vision, sensitive echolocation ([precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411)) 120 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +13 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** -3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -1 **Deep Breath** The drthak can [hold its breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=2436) for about 2 hours.
**Sensitive Echolocation** The drthak can use its hearing as a [precise sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) with the listed range. If the drthak takes sonic damage beyond its resistance, its senses are overloaded and all creatures are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) from it for 1 round. --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +17 **Will** +11 **HP** 110 **Immunities** [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) **Auditory Hunter** **Trigger** A creature within 15 feet uses an [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) action; **Effect** The drthak Strides or [Swims](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) towards the creature. --- **Speed** 20 feet, swim 40 feet **Melee** jaws +16, **Damage** 2d8+4 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Ranged** screech +17 ([range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)), **Damage** 4d6 sonic plus resonant jaws **Bubble Burst** ([Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) **Requirement** The drthak is underwater; **Effect** The drthak uses sonic power to push aside water in a 30-foot emanation, which then collapses in a crushing torrent. All creatures in the area, other than the drthak, take 3d10 bludgeoning damage (DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). **Pull Under** The drthak can [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) at half Speed while it has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) in its jaws, carrying the creature along with it. **Resonant Jaws** When the drthak misses a screech Strike against a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) in its jaws, it deals that creature 2d6 sonic damage.
name: Drthak

<title level="1" pfs="">[Soniphak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3269)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Often mistaken for drakes, soniphaks glide above rocky or barren ground, picking out prey with their hearing. A hunting soniphak disables its targets with concentrated bursts of sound before snatching them up and feasting on the move. Despite being able to speak, soniphaks are no more social than other dischorans, generally responding to even the quietest attempts at communication with destructive shouting.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**

This disrupts a triggering [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) action if the Strike is a critical hit.

Creatures in the area take 5d6 piercing damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save), and the area becomes [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) due to fragments of loose stone.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Chaos Falcon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3270)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The extreme weather that gives birth to chaos falcons grants them a mix of abilities not found in other elemental birds. To them, lightning is as natural as fire and heat as comfortable as cold. The variability of their attacks makes them dangerous, even for the well prepared.

 Like their elemental cousins, chaos falcons are often the harbingers of storms. They're also seen as omens of volcanic activity, as they will travel across great distances to play and dance in the resulting eruption. Between this association and their tendency to rain both fire and ice, their presence is considered dangerous by nearby settlements.

 Fortunately, chaos falcons are solitary birds that prefer to stay away from the noise of civilization. They nest in volcanic vents or areas with geothermal activity, relying on the heat of the earth to incubate their eggs while hunting in the wilderness nearby. Unless their peace is disrupted or their safety threatened, they're unlikely to attack creatures that move through their habitat.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 32

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Chaos_Falcon.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>

<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=527" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=547" />

</traits>

**Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), stormsight

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +19, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +19

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +7

**Con** +3

**Int** +4

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Stormsight** Wind, precipitation, and clouds don't impair a chaos falcon's vision; they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from storms, mist, precipitation, and the like.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 29 

**Fort** +19 

**Ref** +22 

**Will** +16 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 180

</row>

**Resistances**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) 10, [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10

**Storm Nexus** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) 15 feet, 3d6 fire, DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The chaos falcon is surrounded by a cloud of volcanic ash, lightning arcing within the cloud as it glows hot in some areas and collects ice in others. A chaos falcon can change the damage type of this aura to cold, electricity, or fire as an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
beak +21 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 3d10+10 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
talon +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 3d6+10 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bolt +23 ([range 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 6d6 fire plus storm bolt

**Dive-bomb** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Requirements** The chaos falcon is [Flying](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312); **Effect** The chaos falcon Flies twice straight down. If they reach the ground or a similarly solid object at the end of this movement, their landing shatters that surface into sharp shards and droplets of molten stone deal 4d6 fire and 3d8 piercing damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save) to all other creatures within 20 feet.

**Flash Storm** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) Water condenses around the ash particles in the chaos falcon's 15-foot aura, pelting everything beneath it with driving rain. Creatures in that area must succeed at a DC 26 Reflex save or be [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 20 feet down, falling [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) if they start or end this movement on the ground.

**Storm Bolt**   The chaos falcon's bolt Strike always deals the damage type of their storm nexus aura.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Rift Chameleon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3271)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Rift chameleons are best known for their unsettling ability to swallow creatures significantly larger than themselves, thanks to a digestive tract that connects to its own pocket of the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34). When two or more rift chameleons remain in proximity for extended periods of time, their partially extradimensional nature begins to subtly warp the surrounding terrain. Direction and distance become confused to all but the lizards, making it easy for locals to get lost in places they once thought familiar.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 20

**[Recall Knowledge - Ethereal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<image src="/images/Monsters/Rift_Chameleon.webp" />
</column>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); can't speak any language

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +8

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +2

**Con** +3

**Int** -2

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Ethereal Camouflage** A rift chameleon can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404), even if it doesn't have [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372), but it cannot be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) from creatures on the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34).

</column>

---

**AC** 19 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49)

**Fort** +10

**Will** +8

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />

**Damage** 1d8+4 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tongue +11 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** tongue pull

**Extradimensional Gullet**  ([Extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=596)) The rift chameleon's innards exist partially on the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34), allowing it to swallow any number of creatures, even ones larger than itself. Creatures that [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) or cut themselves free from the chameleon's stomach reappear in [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) adjacent to the rift chameleon's position. Creatures cannot escape from the rift chameleon's gullet using [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) effects unless those effects can also cross planar boundaries.

**Flickering Dash** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The rift chameleon Strides twice. During this movement, it flits quickly between [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) and the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34), gaining resistance 5 to physical damage.

**[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) Large, 1d12+2 bludgeoning, Rupture 10

**Tongue Pull**   Any creature hit by the rift chameleon's tongue is pulled adjacent to the chameleon. The creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the next Strike the rift chameleon makes against it this round.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Ghost Ape](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3272)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Ghost apes are reclusive and territorial creatures that usually reveal themselves only when their homes are threatened. Most ghost apes will first attempt to use their imposing presence and natural teleportation to frighten and confuse their foes into fleeing. Ghost apes can speak but aren't particularly talkative, though they will, on rare occasions, treat with those who approach them with respect. Ghost apes can be powerful allies when motivated by a common cause, although they tend to be suspicious of outsiders, and their trust isn't easily earned.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 21

**[Recall Knowledge - Ethereal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 21

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16
</column>

</column>

<image src="/images/Monsters/Ghost_Ape.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>

<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=547" />
<trait label="Ethereal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=593" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 146

**Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +10, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +12, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +12

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +2

**Con** +3

**Int** -1

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 20 

**Fort** +14 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +12 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 65

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +14,
**Damage** 2d8+5 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 1d10+5 bludgeoning

**Ghost Stance** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Stance](/Traits.aspx?ID=701), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The ghost ape phases partially out of existence, its form blurring and becoming difficult to pinpoint. While in this stance, they are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) to any creature that isn't adjacent to them. They can't use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405). They also gain resistance 5 to physical damage. If the ghost ape takes force damage, this stance ends and they can't reenter it for 1d3 rounds.

**Phantom Step** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The ghost ape moves quickly, passing through the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34). They teleport up to a distance equal to twice their Speed within their line of sight.

**Terrifying Display** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The ghost ape beats their chest in a terrifying display. Creatures within 30 feet must succeed a DC 20 Will save or become [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) (or frightened 2 on a critical failure). While a creature is frightened by this ability, it's [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the ape. On a success, the creature is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

</column>
